units.text: Loud sounds of a storm with thunder and lightning. A ship’s MASTER and BOATSWAIN enter.

units.speaker: MASTER
units.text: Boatswain!

units.speaker: BOATSWAIN
units.text: Here, master. What cheer?

units.speaker: MASTER
units.text:
Good, speak to th' mariners. Fall to ’t yarely, or we
run ourselves aground. Bestir, bestir.
The MASTER exits.
SAILORS enter.

units.speaker: BOATSWAIN
units.text:
Heigh, my hearts! Cheerly, cheerly, my hearts! Yare!
Yare!
Take in the topsail. Tend to th' master’s whistle.
Blow, till thou burst thy wind, if room enough!
ALONSO, SEBASTIAN, ANTONIO, FERDINAND, GONZALO, and others enter.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text:
Good Boatswain, have care. Where’s the Master?
Play the men.

units.speaker: BOATSWAIN
units.text: I pray now, keep below.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: Where is the Master, Boatswain?

units.speaker: BOATSWAIN
units.text:
Do you not hear him? You mar our labor. Keep your
cabins.
You do assist the storm.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text: Nay, good, be patient.

units.speaker: BOATSWAIN
units.text:
When the sea is. Hence! What cares these roarers for
the name of king? To cabin, silence! Trouble us not.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text: Good, yet remember whom thou hast aboard.

units.speaker: BOATSWAIN
units.text:
None that I more love than myself. You are a councilor.
If you can command these elements to silence and work
the peace of the present, we will not hand a rope more.
Use your authority. If you cannot, give thanks you have
lived so long and make yourself ready in your cabin for
the mischance of the hour, if it so hap.— Cheerly, good
hearts!— Out of our way, I say.
The BOATSWAIN exits.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
I have great comfort from this fellow. Methinks he hath
no drowning mark upon him. His complexion is perfect
gallows. Stand fast, good Fate, to his hanging. Make the
rope of his destiny our cable, for our own doth little
advantage. If he be not born to be hanged, our case is
miserable.
GONZALO exits with the other men of the royal court.
The BOATSWAIN enters.

units.speaker: BOATSWAIN
units.text:
Down with the topmast! Yare, lower, lower! Bring her to
try wi' th' main course.
A shout offstage.

units.speaker: BOATSWAIN
units.text:
A plague upon this howling! They are louder than the
weather or our office.
SEBASTIAN, ANTONIO, and GONZALO enter.

units.speaker: BOATSWAIN
units.text:
Yet again?
What do you here? Shall we give o'er and drown?
Have you a mind to sink?

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
A pox o' your throat, you bawling, blasphemous,
incharitable dog!

units.speaker: BOATSWAIN
units.text: Work you, then.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
Hang, cur! Hang, you whoreson insolent noisemaker! We
are less afraid to be drowned than thou art.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
I’ll warrant him for drowning though the ship were no
stronger than a nutshell and as leaky as an unstanched
wench.

units.speaker: BOATSWAIN
units.text:
Lay her a-hold, a-hold! Set her two courses off to sea
again.
Lay her off!
Wet SAILORS enter

units.speaker: MARINERS
units.text: All lost! To prayers, to prayers, all lost!
The SAILORS exit.

units.speaker: BOATSWAIN
units.text: What, must our mouths be cold?

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
The king and prince at prayers. Let’s assist them, for
our case is as theirs.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: I’m out of patience.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
We are merely cheated of our lives by drunkards. This
wide-chopped rascal—would thou mightst lie drowning the
washing of ten tides!

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
He’ll be hanged yet, though every drop of water swear
against it and gape at widest to glut him.

units.speaker: VOICES
units.text:
[within] Mercy on us! We split, we split! Farewell, my
wife and children! Farewell, brother! We split, we
split, we split!

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: Let’s all sink wi' th' king.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: Let’s take leave of him.
ANTONIO and SEBASTIAN exit.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
Now would I give a thousand furlongs of sea for an acre
of barren ground: long heath, brown furze, anything.
The wills above be done, but I would fain die a dry
death.
They exit.
PROSPERO and MIRANDA enter.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
If by your art, my dearest father, you have
Put the wild waters in this roar, allay them.
The sky, it seems, would pour down stinking pitch,
But that the sea, mounting to th' welkin’s cheek,
Dashes the fire out. Oh, I have suffered
With those that I saw suffer. A brave vessel
Who had, no doubt, some noble creature in her
Dashed all to pieces. Oh, the cry did knock
Against my very heart! Poor souls, they perished.
Had I been any god of power, I would
Have sunk the sea within the earth or ere
It should the good ship so have swallowed and
The fraughting souls within her.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Be collected.
No more amazement. Tell your piteous heart
There’s no harm done.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text: Oh, woe the day!

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
No harm.
I have done nothing but in care of thee,
Of thee, my dear one—thee my daughter, who
Art ignorant of what thou art, naught knowing
Of whence I am, nor that I am more better
Than Prospero, master of a full poor cell
And thy no greater father.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
More to know
Did never meddle with my thoughts.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
'Tis time
I should inform thee farther. Lend thy hand
And pluck my magic garment from me.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Lie
there, my art. Wipe thou thine eyes. Have comfort.
The direful spectacle of the wrack, which touched
The very virtue of compassion in thee,
I have with such provision in mine art
So safely ordered that there is no soul—
No, not so much perdition as an hair
Betid to any creature in the vessel—
Which thou heard’st cry, which thou sawst sink. Sit
down.
For thou must now know farther.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
You have often
Begun to tell me what I am, but stopped
And left me to a bootless inquisition,
Concluding, “Stay. Not yet.”

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
The hour’s now come.
The very minute bids thee ope thine ear.
Obey and be attentive. Canst thou remember
A time before we came unto this cell?
I do not think thou canst, for then thou wast not
Out three years old.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text: Certainly, sir, I can.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
By what? By any other house or person?
Of anything the image tell me that
Hath kept with thy remembrance.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
'Tis far off,
And rather like a dream than an assurance
That my remembrance warrants. Had I not
Four or five women once that tended me?

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Thou hadst, and more, Miranda. But how is it
That this lives in thy mind? What seest thou else
In the dark backward and abysm of time?
If thou rememberest aught ere thou camest here,
How thou camest here thou mayst.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text: But that I do not.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Twelve year since, Miranda, twelve year since,
Thy father was the Duke of Milan and
A prince of power.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text: Sir, are not you my father?

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Thy mother was a piece of virtue and
She said thou wast my daughter. And thy father
Was Duke of Milan, and thou his only heir
And princess no worse issued.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
Oh, the heavens!
What foul play had we that we came from thence?
Or blessèd was ’t we did?

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Both, both, my girl.
By foul play, as thou sayst, were we heaved thence,
But blessedly holp hither.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
Oh, my heart bleeds
To think o' th' teen that I have turned you to,
Which is from my remembrance! Please you, farther.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
My brother and thy uncle, called Antonio—
I pray thee, mark me (that a brother should
Be so perfidious!)— he whom next thyself
Of all the world I loved and to him put
The manage of my state, as at that time
Through all the signories it was the first,
And Prospero the prime duke, being so reputed
In dignity, and for the liberal arts
Without a parallel. Those being all my study,
The government I cast upon my brother
And to my state grew stranger, being transported
And rapt in secret studies. Thy false uncle—
Dost thou attend me?

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text: Sir, most heedfully.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Being once perfected how to grant suits,
How to deny them, who t' advance and who
To trash for overtopping, new created
The creatures that were mine, I say —or changed 'em,
Or else new formed 'em—having both the key
Of officer and office, set all hearts i' th' state
To what tune pleased his ear, that now he was
The ivy which had hid my princely trunk,
And sucked my verdure out on ’t. Thou attend’st not.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text: O, good sir, I do.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
I pray thee, mark me.
I, thus neglecting worldly ends, all dedicated
To closeness and the bettering of my mind
With that which, but by being so retired,
O'erprized all popular rate, in my false brother
Awaked an evil nature. And my trust,
Like a good parent, did beget of him
A falsehood in its contrary as great
As my trust was, which had indeed no limit,
A confidence sans bound. He being thus lorded,
Not only with what my revenue yielded
But what my power might else exact, like one
Who having into truth, by telling of it,
Made such a sinner of his memory
To credit his own lie— he did believe
He was indeed the duke, out o' th' substitution
And executing th' outward face of royalty,
With all prerogative: Hence his ambition growing—
Dost thou hear?

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text: Your tale, sir, would cure deafness.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
To have no screen between this part he played
And him he played it for, he needs will be
Absolute Milan. Me, poor man, my library
Was dukedom large enough. Of temporal royalties
He thinks me now incapable, confederates—
So dry he was for sway—wi' th' King of Naples
To give him annual tribute, do him homage,
Subject his coronet to his crown and bend
The dukedom yet unbowed— alas, poor Milan!—
To most ignoble stooping.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text: Oh, the heavens!

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Mark his condition and the event. Then tell me
If this might be a brother.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
I should sin
To think but nobly of my grandmother.
Good wombs have borne bad sons.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Now the condition.
The King of Naples, being an enemy
To me inveterate, hearkens my brother’s suit,
Which was that he, in lieu o' th' premises
Of homage and I know not how much tribute,
Should presently extirpate me and mine
Out of the dukedom, and confer fair Milan
With all the honors on my brother. Whereon,
A treacherous army levied, one midnight
Fated to th' purpose did Antonio open
The gates of Milan, and, i' th' dead of darkness,
The ministers for th' purpose hurried thence
Me and thy crying self.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
Alack, for pity!
I, not remembering how I cried out then,
Will cry it o'er again. It is a hint
That wrings mine eyes to ’t.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Hear a little further
And then I’ll bring thee to the present business
Which now ’s upon ’s, without the which this story
Were most impertinent.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
Wherefore did they not
That hour destroy us?

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Well demanded, wench.
My tale provokes that question. Dear, they durst not,
So dear the love my people bore me, nor set
A mark so bloody on the business, but
With colors fairer painted their foul ends.
In few, they hurried us aboard a bark,
Bore us some leagues to sea, where they prepared
A rotten carcass of a butt, not rigged,
Nor tackle, sail, nor mast. The very rats
Instinctively had quit it. There they hoist us
To cry to th' sea that roared to us, to sigh
To th' winds whose pity, sighing back again,
Did us but loving wrong.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
Alack, what trouble
Was I then to you!

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Oh, a cherubim
Thou wast that did preserve me. Thou didst smile
Infusèd with a fortitude from heaven,
When I have decked the sea with drops full salt,
Under my burthen groaned; which raised in me
An undergoing stomach to bear up
Against what should ensue.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text: How came we ashore?

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
By providence divine.
Some food we had and some fresh water that
A noble Neapolitan, Gonzalo,
Out of his charity, who being then appointed
Master of this design, did give us, with
Rich garments, linens, stuffs, and necessaries,
Which since have steaded much. So, of his gentleness,
Knowing I loved my books, he furnished me
From mine own library with volumes that
I prize above my dukedom.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
Would I might
But ever see that man!

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Now I arise.
[stands and puts on his mantle]
Sit still, and hear the last of our sea-sorrow.
Here in this island we arrived, and here
Have I, thy schoolmaster, made thee more profit
Than other princesses can that have more time
For vainer hours and tutors not so careful.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
Heavens thank you for ’t! And now, I pray you, sir—
For still ’tis beating in my mind—your reason
For raising this sea storm?

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Know thus far forth:
By accident most strange, bountiful Fortune
(Now my dear lady) hath mine enemies
Brought to this shore. And by my prescience
I find my zenith doth depend upon
A most auspicious star, whose influence
If now I court not but omit, my fortunes
Will ever after droop. Here cease more questions.
Thou art inclined to sleep. 'Tis a good dullness,
And give it way. I know thou canst not choose.
MIRANDA falls asleep.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Come
away, servant, come. I am ready now.
Approach, my Ariel, come.
ARIEL enters.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text:
All hail, great master! Grave sir, hail! I come
To answer thy best pleasure, be ’t to fly,
To swim, to dive into the fire, to ride
On the curled clouds. To thy strong bidding, task
Ariel and all his quality.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Hast thou, spirit,
Performed to point the tempest that I bade thee?

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text:
To every article.
I boarded the king’s ship. Now on the beak,
Now in the waist, the deck, in every cabin,
I flamed amazement. Sometime I’d divide,
And burn in many places. On the topmast,
The yards, and bowsprit would I flame distinctly,
Then meet and join. Jove’s lightning, the precursors
O' th' dreadful thunderclaps, more momentary
And sight-outrunning were not. The fire and cracks
Of sulfurous roaring the most mighty Neptune
Seem to besiege and make his bold waves tremble,
Yea, his dread trident shake.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
My brave spirit!
Who was so firm, so constant, that this coil
Would not infect his reason?

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text:
Not a soul
But felt a fever of the mad and played
Some tricks of desperation. All but mariners
Plunged in the foaming brine and quit the vessel,
Then all afire with me. The king’s son, Ferdinand,
With hair up-staring—then, like reeds, not hair—
Was the first man that leaped, cried, “Hell is empty
And all the devils are here.”

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Why, that’s my spirit!
But was not this nigh shore?

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: Close by, my master.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text: But are they, Ariel, safe?

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text:
Not a hair perished.
On their sustaining garments not a blemish,
But fresher than before. And, as thou badest me,
In troops I have dispersed them 'bout the isle.
The king’s son have I landed by himself,
Whom I left cooling of the air with sighs
In an odd angle of the isle, and sitting,
His arms in this sad knot.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Of the king’s ship,
The mariners, say how thou hast disposed,
And all the rest o' th' fleet.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text:
Safely in harbor
Is the king’s ship. In the deep nook where once
Thou called’st me up at midnight to fetch dew
From the still-vexed Bermoothes, there she’s hid.
The mariners all under hatches stowed,
Who, with a charm joined to their suffered labor,
I have left asleep. And for the rest o' th' fleet,
Which I dispersed, they all have met again
And are upon the Mediterranean float,
Bound sadly home for Naples,
Supposing that they saw the king’s ship wracked
And his great person perish.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Ariel, thy charge
Exactly is performed. But there’s more work.
What is the time o' th' day?

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: Past the mid season.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
At least two glasses. The time ’twixt six and now
Must by us both be spent most preciously.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text:
Is there more toil? Since thou dost give me pains,
Let me remember thee what thou hast promised,
Which is not yet performed me.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
How now? Moody?
What is ’t thou canst demand?

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: My liberty.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text: Before the time be out? No more!

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text:
I prithee,
Remember I have done thee worthy service,
Told thee no lies, made thee no mistakings, served
Without or grudge or grumblings. Thou didst promise
To bate me a full year.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Dost thou forget
From what a torment I did free thee?

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: No.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Thou dost, and think’st it much to tread the ooze
Of the salt deep,
To run upon the sharp wind of the north,
To do me business in the veins o' th' earth
When it is baked with frost.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: I do not, sir.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Thou liest, malignant thing! Hast thou forgot
The foul witch Sycorax, who with age and envy
Was grown into a hoop? Hast thou forgot her?

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: No, sir.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text: Thou hast. Where was she born? Speak. Tell me.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: Sir, in Argier.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Oh, was she so? I must
Once in a month recount what thou hast been,
Which thou forget’st. This damned witch Sycorax,
For mischiefs manifold and sorceries terrible
To enter human hearing, from Argier,
Thou know’st, was banished. For one thing she did
They would not take her life. Is not this true?

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: Ay, sir.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
This blue-eyed hag was hither brought with child
And here was left by th' sailors. Thou, my slave,
As thou report’st thyself, wast then her servant.
And, for thou wast a spirit too delicate
To act her earthy and abhorred commands,
Refusing her grand hests, she did confine thee,
By help of her more potent ministers
And in her most unmitigable rage,
Into a cloven pine, within which rift
Imprisoned thou didst painfully remain
A dozen years; within which space she died
And left thee there, where thou didst vent thy groans
As fast as mill wheels strike. Then was this island—
Save for the son that she did litter here,
A freckled whelp hag-born—not honored with
A human shape.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: Yes, Caliban, her son.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Dull thing, I say so. He, that Caliban
Whom now I keep in service. Thou best know’st
What torment I did find thee in. Thy groans
Did make wolves howl and penetrate the breasts
Of ever angry bears. It was a torment
To lay upon the damned, which Sycorax
Could not again undo. It was mine art,
When I arrived and heard thee, that made gape
The pine and let thee out.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: I thank thee, master.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
If thou more murmur’st, I will rend an oak
And peg thee in his knotty entrails till
Thou hast howled away twelve winters.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text:
Pardon, master.
I will be correspondent to command
And do my spiriting gently.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Do so, and after two days
I will discharge thee.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text:
That’s my noble master!
What shall I do? Say, what? What shall I do?

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Go make thyself like a nymph o' th' sea. Be subject
To no sight but thine and mine, invisible
To every eyeball else. Go take this shape
And hither come in ’t. Go hence with diligence.
ARIEL exits.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
[to MIRANDA]
Awake, dear heart, awake! Thou hast slept well.
Awake!

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
[waking] The strangeness of your story put
Heaviness in me.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Shake it off. Come on.
We’ll visit Caliban, my slave who never
Yields us kind answer.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
'Tis a villain, sir,
I do not love to look on.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
But as ’tis,
We cannot miss him. He does make our fire,
Fetch in our wood, and serves in offices
That profit us. What, ho! Slave! Caliban!
Thou earth, thou! Speak.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text: [within] There’s wood enough within.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Come forth, I say! There’s other business for thee.
Come, thou tortoise! When?
ARIEL enters, in the form of a water nymph.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Fine apparition! My quaint Ariel,
Hark in thine ear. [whispers to ARIEL]

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: My lord it shall be done.
ARIEL exits.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
[to CALIBAN] Thou poisonous slave, got by the devil
himself
Upon thy wicked dam, come forth!
CALIBAN enters.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
As wicked dew as e'er my mother brushed
With raven’s feather from unwholesome fen
Drop on you both! A southwest blow on ye
And blister you all o'er!

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
For this, be sure, tonight thou shalt have cramps,
Side-stitches that shall pen thy breath up. Urchins
Shall, forth at vast of night that they may work,
All exercise on thee. Thou shalt be pinched
As thick as honeycomb, each pinch more stinging
Than bees that made 'em.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
I must eat my dinner.
This island’s mine, by Sycorax my mother,
Which thou takest from me. When thou camest first,
Thou strok’st me and made much of me, wouldst give me
Water with berries in ’t, and teach me how
To name the bigger light, and how the less,
That burn by day and night. And then I loved thee
And showed thee all the qualities o' th' isle,
The fresh springs, brine pits, barren place and
fertile.
Cursed be I that did so! All the charms
Of Sycorax, toads, beetles, bats, light on you!
For I am all the subjects that you have,
Which first was mine own king. And here you sty me
In this hard rock, whiles you do keep from me
The rest o' th' island.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Thou most lying slave,
Whom stripes may move, not kindness! I have used thee,
Filth as thou art, with human care, and lodged thee
In mine own cell till thou didst seek to violate
The honor of my child.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
Oh ho, oh ho! Would ’t had been done!
Thou didst prevent me. I had peopled else
This isle with Calibans.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
Abhorrèd slave,
Which any print of goodness wilt not take,
Being capable of all ill! I pitied thee,
Took pains to make thee speak, taught thee each hour
One thing or other. When thou didst not, savage,
Know thine own meaning, but wouldst gabble like
A thing most brutish, I endowed thy purposes
With words that made them known. But thy vile race,
Though thou didst learn, had that in ’t which good
natures
Could not abide to be with. Therefore wast thou
Deservedly confined into this rock,
Who hadst deserved more than a prison.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
You taught me language, and my profit on ’t
Is I know how to curse. The red plague rid you
For learning me your language!

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Hag-seed, hence!
Fetch us in fuel. And be quick, thou 'rt best,
To answer other business. Shrug’st thou, malice?
If thou neglect’st or dost unwillingly
What I command, I’ll rack thee with old cramps,
Fill all thy bones with aches, make thee roar
That beasts shall tremble at thy din.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
No, pray thee.
[aside] I must obey. His art is of such power,
It would control my dam’s god, Setebos,
And make a vassal of him.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text: So, slave, hence!
CALIBAN exits.
FERDINAND enters with ARIEL, who is invisible and is playing music and singing.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text:
[sings]
Come unto these yellow sands,
And then take hands.
Curtsied when you have, and kissed
The wild waves whist.
Foot it featly here and there,
And, sweet sprites, bear
The burden. Hark, hark!

units.speaker: SPIRITS
units.text: [dispersedly, within] Bow-wow.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: The watchdogs bark.

units.speaker: SPIRITS
units.text: (within) Bow-wow.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text:
Hark, hark! I hear
The strain of strutting chanticleer
Cry “Cock-a-diddle-dow.”

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
Where should this music be? I' th' air or th' earth?
It sounds no more, and sure, it waits upon
Some god o' th' island. Sitting on a bank,
Weeping again the king my father’s wrack,
This music crept by me upon the waters,
Allaying both their fury and my passion
With its sweet air. Thence I have followed it,
Or it hath drawn me rather. But ’tis gone.
No, it begins again.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text:
[sings]
Full fathom five thy father lies.
Of his bones are coral made.
Those are pearls that were his eyes.
Nothing of him that doth fade,
But doth suffer a sea-change
Into something rich and strange.
Sea nymphs hourly ring his knell.

units.speaker: SPIRITS
units.text: [within] Ding-dong.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: Hark, now I hear them.

units.speaker: SPIRITS
units.text: [within] Ding-dong, bell.

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
The ditty does remember my drowned father.
This is no mortal business, nor no sound
That the earth owes. I hear it now above me.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
[to MIRANDA] The fringèd curtains of thine eye advance
And say what thou seest yond.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
What is ’t? A spirit?
Lord, how it looks about! Believe me, sir,
It carries a brave form. But ’tis a spirit.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
No, wench! It eats and sleeps and hath such senses
As we have, such. This gallant which thou seest
Was in the wrack. And, but he’s something stained
With grief that’s beauty’s canker, thou mightst call
him
A goodly person. He hath lost his fellows
And strays about to find 'em.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
I might call him
A thing divine, for nothing natural
I ever saw so noble.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
[aside] It goes on, I see,
As my soul prompts it. Spirit, fine spirit! I’ll free
thee
Within two days for this.

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
[seeing MIRANDA] Most sure, the goddess
On whom these airs attend! Vouchsafe my prayer
May know if you remain upon this island,
And that you will some good instruction give
How I may bear me here. My prime request,
Which I do last pronounce, is, O you wonder!
If you be maid or no.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
No wonder, sir,
But certainly a maid.

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
My language! Heavens,
I am the best of them that speak this speech,
Were I but where ’tis spoken.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
How? The best?
What wert thou if the King of Naples heard thee?

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
A single thing, as I am now, that wonders
To hear thee speak of Naples. He does hear me,
And that he does I weep. Myself am Naples,
Who with mine eyes, never since at ebb, beheld
The king my father wracked.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text: Alack, for mercy!

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
Yes, faith, and all his lords, the Duke of Milan
And his brave son being twain.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
[aside] The Duke of Milan
And his more braver daughter could control thee
If now ’twere fit to do ’t! At the first sight
They have changed eyes. Delicate Ariel,
I’ll set thee free for this.
[to FERDINAND]
A word, good sir.
I fear you have done yourself some wrong. A word.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
[ aside] Why speaks my father so ungently? This
Is the third man that e'er I saw, the first
That e'er I sighed for. Pity move my father
To be inclined my way!

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
[to MIRANDA]
Oh, if a virgin,
And your affection not gone forth, I’ll make you
The queen of Naples.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Soft, sir! One word more.
[ aside]
They are both in either’s powers, but this swift
business
I must uneasy make lest too light winning
Make the prize light.
[To FERDINAND]
One word more. I charge thee
That thou attend me. Thou dost here usurp
The name thou owest not, and hast put thyself
Upon this island as a spy to win it
From me, the lord on ’t.

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text: No, as I am a man!

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
There’s nothing ill can dwell in such a temple.
If the ill spirit have so fair a house,
Good things will strive to dwell with ’t.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
[to FERDINAND] Follow me.
[to MIRANDA] Speak not you for him. He’s a traitor.
[to FERDINAND] Come,
I’ll manacle thy neck and feet together.
Seawater shalt thou drink. Thy food shall be
The fresh-brook muscles, withered roots, and husks
Wherein the acorn cradled. Follow.

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
No.
I will resist such entertainment till
Mine enemy has more power.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
O dear father,
Make not too rash a trial of him, for
He’s gentle and not fearful.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
What, I say?
My foot my tutor?— Put thy sword up, traitor,
Who makest a show but darest not strike, thy conscience
Is so possessed with guilt. Come from thy ward,
For I can here disarm thee with this stick
And make thy weapon drop.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text: Beseech you, father.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text: Hence! Hang not on my garments.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
Sir, have pity,
I’ll be his surety.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Silence! One word more
Shall make me chide thee, if not hate thee. What,
An advocate for an imposter? Hush,
Thou think’st there is no more such shapes as he,
Having seen but him and Caliban. Foolish wench,
To th' most of men this is a Caliban
And they to him are angels.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
My affections
Are then most humble. I have no ambition
To see a goodlier man.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
[to FERDINAND] Come on. Obey.
Thy nerves are in their infancy again
And have no vigor in them.

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
So they are.
My spirits, as in a dream, are all bound up.
My father’s loss, the weakness which I feel,
The wrack of all my friends, nor this man’s threats,
To whom I am subdued, are but light to me,
Might I but through my prison once a day
Behold this maid. All corners else o' th' earth
Let liberty make use of. Space enough
Have I in such a prison.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
[aside] It works!
[to FERDINAND] Come on.
[aside] Thou hast done well, fine Ariel!
[to FERDINAND] Follow me.
[to ARIEL] Hark what thou else shalt do me.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
[to FERDINAND] Be of comfort.
My father’s of a better nature, sir,
Than he appears by speech. This is unwonted
Which now came from him.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
[to ARIEL] Thou shalt be free
As mountain winds. But then exactly do
All points of my command.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: To th' syllable.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
[to FERDINAND] Come, follow.
[to MIRANDA] Speak not for him.
They exit.
ALONSO, SEBASTIAN, ANTONIO, GONZALO, ADRIAN, FRANCISCO, and others enter.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
[to ALONSO]
Beseech you, sir, be merry. You have cause,
So have we all, of joy, for our escape
Is much beyond our loss. Our hint of woe
Is common. Every day some sailor’s wife,
The masters of some merchant, and the merchant
Have just our theme of woe. But for the miracle—
I mean our preservation—few in millions
Can speak like us. Then wisely, good sir, weigh
Our sorrow with our comfort.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text: Prithee, peace.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: [to ANTONIO] He receives comfort like cold porridge.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: [to SEBASTIAN] The visitor will not give him o'er so.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
Look he’s winding up the watch of his wit. By and by it
will strike.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text: [to ALONSO] Sir—

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: [to ANTONIO] One. Tell.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
When every grief is entertained that’s offered,
Comes to th' entertainer—

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: A dollar.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
Dolor comes to him, indeed. You have spoken truer than
you purposed.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: You have taken it wiselier than I meant you should.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text: [to ALONSO] Therefore, my lord—

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
[to SEBASTIAN] Fie, what a spendthrift is he of his
tongue!

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text: [to GONZALO] I prithee, spare.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text: Well, I have done. But yet—

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: [to ANTONIO] He will be talking.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
Which, of he or Adrian, for a good wager, first begins
to crow?

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: The old cock.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: The cockerel.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: Done. The wager?

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: A laughter.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: A match!

units.speaker: ADRIAN
units.text: Though this island seem to be desert—

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: [to SEBASTIAN] Ha, ha, ha!

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: So you’re paid.

units.speaker: ADRIAN
units.text: Uninhabitable and almost inaccessible—

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: Yet—

units.speaker: ADRIAN
units.text: Yet—

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: He could not miss ’t.

units.speaker: ADRIAN
units.text:
It must needs be of subtle, tender, and delicate
temperance.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: Temperance was a delicate wench.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: Ay, and a subtle, as he most learnedly delivered.

units.speaker: ADRIAN
units.text: The air breathes upon us here most sweetly.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: As if it had lungs, and rotten ones.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: Or as ’twere perfumed by a fen.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text: Here is everything advantageous to life.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: True. Save means to live.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: Of that there’s none, or little.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text: How lush and lusty the grass looks! How green!

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: The ground indeed is tawny.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: With an eye of green in ’t.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: He misses not much.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: No, he doth but mistake the truth totally.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
But the rarity of it is—which is indeed almost beyond
credit—

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: As many vouched rarities are.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
That our garments, being, as they were, drenched in the
sea, hold notwithstanding their freshness and glosses,
being rather new-dyed than stained with salt water.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
If but one of his pockets could speak, would it not say
he lies?

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: Ay, or very falsely pocket up his report.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
Methinks our garments are now as fresh as when we put
them on first in Afric, at the marriage of the king’s
fair daughter Claribel to the King of Tunis.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
'Twas a sweet marriage, and we prosper well in our
return.

units.speaker: ADRIAN
units.text:
Tunis was never graced before with such a paragon to
their queen.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text: Not since widow Dido’s time.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
Widow! A pox o' that! How came that “widow” in? Widow
Dido!

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
What if he had said “widower Aeneas” too? Good Lord,
how you take it!

units.speaker: ADRIAN
units.text:
“Widow Dido” said you? You make me study of that. She
was of Carthage, not of Tunis.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text: This Tunis, sir, was Carthage.

units.speaker: ADRIAN
units.text: Carthage?

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text: I assure you, Carthage.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
His word is more than the miraculous harp. He hath
raised the wall and houses too.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: What impossible matter will he make easy next?

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
I think he will carry this island home in his pocket
and give it his son for an apple.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
And sowing the kernels of it in the sea, bring forth
more islands.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text: Ay.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: Why, in good time.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
[to ALONSO] Sir, we were talking that our garments seem
now as fresh as when we were at Tunis at the marriage
of your daughter, who is now queen.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: And the rarest that e'er came there.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: Bate, I beseech you, widow Dido.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: Oh, widow Dido? Ay, widow Dido.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
Is not, sir, my doublet as fresh as the first day I
wore it? I mean, in a sort.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: That “sort” was well fished for.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text: When I wore it at your daughter’s marriage?

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text:
You cram these words into mine ears against
The stomach of my sense. Would I had never
Married my daughter there! For, coming thence,
My son is lost and, in my rate, she too,
Who is so far from Italy removed
I ne'er again shall see her. O thou mine heir
Of Naples and of Milan, what strange fish
Hath made his meal on thee?

units.speaker: FRANCISCO
units.text:
Sir, he may live.
I saw him beat the surges under him,
And ride upon their backs. He trod the water,
Whose enmity he flung aside, and breasted
The surge most swoll'n that met him. His bold head
'Bove the contentious waves he kept, and oared
Himself with his good arms in lusty stroke
To th' shore, that o'er his wave-worn basis bowed,
As stooping to relieve him. I not doubt
He came alive to land.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text: No, no, he’s gone.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
Sir, you may thank yourself for this great loss,
That would not bless our Europe with your daughter,
But rather loose her to an African,
Where she at least is banished from your eye,
Who hath cause to wet the grief on ’t.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text: Prithee, peace.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
You were kneeled to and importuned otherwise
By all of us, and the fair soul herself
Weighed between loathness and obedience, at
Which end o' th' beam should bow. We have lost your
son,
I fear, forever. Milan and Naples have
More widows in them of this business' making
Than we bring men to comfort them.
The fault’s your own.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text: So is the dearest o' th' loss.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
My lord Sebastian,
The truth you speak doth lack some gentleness
And time to speak it in. You rub the sore
When you should bring the plaster.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: Very well.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: And most chirurgeonly.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
[to ALONSO] It is foul weather in us all, good sir,
When you are cloudy.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: Foul weather?

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: Very foul.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text: Had I plantation of this isle, my lord—

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: He’d sow ’t with nettle seed.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: Or docks, or mallows.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text: And were the king on ’t, what would I do?

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: 'Scape being drunk for want of wine.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
I' th' commonwealth I would by contraries
Execute all things. For no kind of traffic
Would I admit. No name of magistrate.
Letters should not be known. Riches, poverty,
And use of service—none. Contract, succession,
Bourn, bound of land, tilth, vineyard—none.
No use of metal, corn, or wine, or oil.
No occupation. All men idle, all.
And women too, but innocent and pure.
No sovereignty—

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: Yet he would be king on ’t.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
The latter end of his commonwealth forgets the
beginning.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
All things in common nature should produce
Without sweat or endeavor. Treason, felony,
Sword, pike, knife, gun, or need of any engine,
Would I not have. But nature should bring forth
Of its own kind all foison, all abundance,
To feed my innocent people.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: No marrying ’mong his subjects?

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: None, man. All idle. Whores and knaves.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
I would with such perfection govern, sir,
T' excel the Golden Age.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: 'Save his majesty!

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: Long live Gonzalo!

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text: [to ALONSO] And—do you mark me, sir?

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text: Prithee, no more. Thou dost talk nothing to me.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
I do well believe your highness, and did it to minister
occasion to these gentlemen, who are of such sensible
and nimble lungs that they always use to laugh at
nothing.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: 'Twas you we laughed at.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
Who in this kind of merry fooling am nothing to you. So
you may continue and laugh at nothing still.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: What a blow was there given!

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: An it had not fallen flat-long.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
You are gentlemen of brave mettle. You would lift the
moon out of her sphere if she would continue in it five
weeks without changing.
ARIEL enters, invisible, playing solemn music.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: We would so, and then go a-batfowling.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: [to GONZALO] Nay, good my lord, be not angry.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
No, I warrant you. I will not adventure my discretion
so weakly. Will you laugh me asleep, for I am very
heavy?

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: Go sleep, and hear us.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text:
What, all so soon asleep? I wish mine eyes
Would with themselves shut up my thoughts. I find
They are inclined to do so.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
Please you, sir,
Do not omit the heavy offer of it.
It seldom visits sorrow. When it doth,
It is a comforter.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
We two, my lord,
Will guard your person while you take your rest
And watch your safety.
Thank you. Wondrous heavy. [falls asleep]
ARIEL exits.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: What a strange drowsiness possesses them!

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: It is the quality o' th' climate.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
Why
Doth it not then our eyelids sink? I find not
Myself disposed to sleep.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
Nor I. My spirits are nimble.
They fell together all, as by consent.
They dropped, as by a thunderstroke. What might,
Worthy Sebastian, O, what might—? No more.—
And yet methinks I see it in thy face,
What thou shouldst be. Th' occasion speaks thee, and
My strong imagination sees a crown
Dropping upon thy head.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: What, art thou waking?

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: Do you not hear me speak?

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
I do, and surely
It is a sleepy language, and thou speak’st
Out of thy sleep. What is it thou didst say?
This is a strange repose, to be asleep
With eyes wide open, standing, speaking, moving,
And yet so fast asleep.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
Noble Sebastian,
Thou let’st thy fortune sleep—die, rather—wink’st
Whiles thou art waking.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
Thou dost snore distinctly.
There’s meaning in thy snores.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
I am more serious than my custom. You
Must be so too if heed me, which to do
Trebles thee o'er.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: Well, I am standing water.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: I’ll teach you how to flow.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
Do so. To ebb
Hereditary sloth instructs me.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
Oh,
If you but knew how you the purpose cherish
Whiles thus you mock it! How, in stripping it,
You more invest it! Ebbing men indeed
Most often do so near the bottom run
By their own fear or sloth.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
Prithee, say on.
The setting of thine eye and cheek proclaim
A matter from thee, and a birth indeed
Which throes thee much to yield.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
Thus, sir:
Although this lord of weak remembrance—this,
Who shall be of as little memory
When he is earthed— hath here almost persuade
(For he’s a spirit of persuasion only,
Professes to persuade) the king his son’s alive,
'Tis as impossible that he’s undrowned
And he that sleeps here swims.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
I have no hope
That he’s undrowned.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
Oh, out of that “no hope”
What great hope have you! No hope that way is
Another way so high a hope that even
Ambition cannot pierce a wink beyond,
But doubt discovery there. Will you grant with me
That Ferdinand is drowned?

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: He’s gone.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
Then, tell me,
Who’s the next heir of Naples?

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: Claribel.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
She that is Queen of Tunis; she that dwells
Ten leagues beyond man’s life; she that from Naples
Can have no note, unless the sun were post—
The man i' th' moon’s too slow— till newborn chins
Be rough and razorable; she that from whom
We all were sea-swallowed, though some cast again,
And by that destiny to perform an act
Whereof what’s past is prologue, what to come
In yours and my discharge.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
What stuff is this? How say you?
'Tis true, my brother’s daughter’s Queen of Tunis,
So is she heir of Naples, ’twixt which regions
There is some space.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
A space whose every cubit
Seems to cry out, “How shall that Claribel
Measure us back to Naples? Keep in Tunis,
And let Sebastian wake.” Say this were death
That now hath seized them. Why, they were no worse
Than now they are. There be that can rule Naples
As well as he that sleeps, lords that can prate
As amply and unnecessarily
As this Gonzalo. I myself could make
A chough of as deep chat. Oh, that you bore
The mind that I do, what a sleep were this
For your advancement! Do you understand me?

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: Methinks I do.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
And how does your content
Tender your own good fortune?

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
I remember
You did supplant your brother Prospero.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
True.
And look how well my garments sit upon me,
Much feater than before. My brother’s servants
Were then my fellows. Now they are my men.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: But, for your conscience?

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
Ay, sir. Where lies that? If ’twere a kibe,
'Twould put me to my slipper. But I feel not
This deity in my bosom. Twenty consciences,
That stand ’twixt me and Milan, candied be they
And melt ere they molest! Here lies your brother,
No better than the earth he lies upon,
If he were that which now he’s like, that’s dead;
Whom I, with this obedient steel, three inches of it,
Can lay to bed for ever; whiles you, doing thus,
To the perpetual wink for aye might put
This ancient morsel, this Sir Prudence, who
Should not upbraid our course. For all the rest,
They’ll take suggestion as a cat laps milk.
They’ll tell the clock to any business that
We say befits the hour.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
Thy case, dear friend,
Shall be my precedent. As thou got’st Milan,
I’ll come by Naples. Draw thy sword. One stroke
Shall free thee from the tribute which thou payest.
And I the king shall love thee.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
Draw together.
And when I rear my hand, do you the like,
To fall it on Gonzalo.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
O, but one word.
[speaks quietly to ANTONIO]
ARIEL enters, invisible, playing music and singing.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text:
[to GONZALO] My master through his art foresees the
danger
That you, his friend, are, and sends me forth—
For else his project dies—to keep them living.
[sings in GONZALO’s ear]
While you here do snoring lie,
Open-eyed conspiracy
His time doth take.
If of life you keep a care,
Shake off slumber and beware.
Awake! Awake!

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: Then let us both be sudden.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
[waking and seeing them]
Now, good angels preserve the king!

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text: [waking] Why, how now? Ho, awake!

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text: What’s the matter?

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
Whiles we stood here securing your repose,
Even now, we heard a hollow burst of bellowing
Like bulls, or rather lions. Did ’t not wake you?
It struck mine ear most terribly.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text: I heard nothing.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
Oh, ’twas a din to fright a monster’s ear,
To make an earthquake! Sure, it was the roar
Of a whole herd of lions.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text: Heard you this, Gonzalo?

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
Upon mine honor, sir, I heard a humming,
And that a strange one too, which did awake me.
I shaked you, sir, and cried. As mine eyes opened,
I saw their weapons drawn. There was a noise,
That’s verily. 'Tis best we stand upon our guard,
Or that we quit this place. Let’s draw our weapons.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text:
Lead off this ground, and let’s make further search
For my poor son.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
Heavens keep him from these beasts!
For he is, sure, i' th' island.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text: Lead away.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text:
[aside] Prospero my lord shall know what I have done.
So, King, go safely on to seek thy son.
They exit.
CALIBAN enters, carrying a load of wood. There is a sound of thunder.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
All the infections that the sun sucks up
From bogs, fens, flats, on Prosper fall and make him
By inchmeal a disease! His spirits hear me
And yet I needs must curse. But they’ll nor pinch,
Fright me with urchin-shows, pitch me i' th' mire,
Nor lead me like a firebrand in the dark
Out of my way, unless he bid 'em. But
For every trifle are they set upon me,
Sometime like apes that mow and chatter at me,
And after bite me, then like hedgehogs which
Lie tumbling in my barefoot way and mount
Their pricks at my footfall. Sometime am I
All wound with adders who with cloven tongues
Do hiss me into madness.
TRINCULO enters.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
Lo, now, lo!
Here comes a spirit of his, and to torment me
For bringing wood in slowly. I’ll fall flat.
Perchance he will not mind me. [lies down, covered by
his gaberdine]

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
Here’s neither bush nor shrub to bear off any weather
at all. And another storm brewing, I hear it sing i' th'
wind. Yond same black cloud, yond huge one, looks like
a foul bombard that would shed his liquor. If it should
thunder as it did before, I know not where to hide my
head. Yond same cloud cannot choose but fall by
pailfuls. [sees CALIBAN] What have we here? A man or a
fish? Dead or alive? A fish. He smells like a fish, a
very ancient and fish-like smell, a kind of
not-of-the-newest poor-john. A strange fish! Were I in
England now, as once I was, and had but this fish
painted, not a holiday fool there but would give a piece
of silver. There would this monster make a man. Any
strange beast there makes a man. When they will not give
a doit to relieve a lame beggar, they will lay out ten
to see a dead Indian. Legged like a man and his fins
like arms! Warm, o' my troth. I do now let loose my
opinion, hold it no longer: this is no fish, but an
islander that hath lately suffered by a thunderbolt.
Thunder.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
Alas,
the storm is come again! My best way is to creep under
his gaberdine. T here is no other shelter hereabouts.
Misery acquaints a man with strange bedfellows. I will
here shroud till the dregs of the storm be past. [crawls
under gaberdine]
STEPHANO enters, singing.

[sings]
I shall no more to sea, to sea,
Here shall I die ashore—
This is a very scurvy tune to sing at a man’s funeral.
Well, here’s my comfort. [drinks, sings]
The master, the swabber, the boatswain, and I,
The gunner and his mate
Loved Mall, Meg, and Marian, and Margery,
But none of us cared for Kate.
For she had a tongue with a tang,
Would cry to a sailor, “Go hang!”
She loved not the savor of tar nor of pitch,
Yet a tailor might scratch her where'ershe did itch.
Then to sea, boys, and let her go hang!
This is a scurvy tune too. But here’s my comfort.
[drinks]

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text: Do not torment me. Oh!

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
What’s the matter? Have we devils here? Do you put
tricks upon ’s with savages and men of Ind, ha? I have
not ’scaped drowning to be afeard now of your four legs.
Or it hath been said, “As proper a man as ever went on
four legs cannot make him give ground,” and it shall be
said so again while Stephano breathes at' nostrils.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text: The spirit torments me. Oh!

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
This is some monster of the isle with four legs who
hath got, as I take it, an ague. Where the devil should
he learn our language? I will give him some relief if it
be but for that. If I can recover him and keep him tame
and get to Naples with him, he’s a present for any
emperor that ever trod on neat’s leather.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
Do not torment me, prithee. I’ll bring my wood home
faster.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
He’s in his fit now and does not talk after the wisest.
He shall taste of my bottle. If he have never drunk
wine afore, it will go near to remove his fit. If I can
recover him and keep him tame, I will not take too much
for him. He shall pay for him that hath him, and that
soundly.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
Thou dost me yet but little hurt. Thou wilt anon, I
know it by thy trembling. Now Prosper works upon thee.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
[trying to give CALIBAN drink]
Come on your ways. Open your mouth. Here is that which
will give language to you, cat. Open your mouth. This
will shake your shaking, I can tell you, and that
soundly. You cannot tell who’s your friend. Open your
chaps again.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
I should know that voice. It should be—But he is
drowned, and these are devils. Oh, defend me!

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
Four legs and two voices—a most delicate monster.His
forward voice now is to speak well of his friend. His
backward voice is to utter foul speeches and to detract.
If all the wine in my bottle will recover him, I will
help his ague. Come. [CALIBAN drinks] Amen! I will pour
some in thy other mouth.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text: Stephano!

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
Doth thy other mouth call me? Mercy, mercy! This is a
devil, and no monster. I will leave him. I have no long
spoon.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
Stephano! If thou beest Stephano, touch me and speak to
me. For I am Trinculo—be not afeard—thy good friend
Trinculo.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
If thou beest Trinculo, come forth. I’ll pull thee by
the lesser legs. If any be Trinculo’s legs, these are
they. [pulls TRINCULO out from under the gaberdine] Thou
art very Trinculo indeed! How camest thou to be the
siege of this mooncalf? Can he vent Trinculos?

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
I took him to be killed with a thunderstroke. But art
thou not drowned, Stephano? I hope now thou art not
drowned. Is the storm overblown? I hid me under the dead
mooncalf’s gaberdine for fear of the storm. And art
thou living, Stephano? O Stephano, two Neapolitans
’scaped! [dances STEPHANO about]

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
Prithee, do not turn me about. My stomach is not
constant.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
[aside] These be fine things, an if they be not
sprites. That’s a brave god and bears celestial liquor.
I will kneel to him.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
[to TRINCULO] How didst thou ’scape? How camest thou
hither? Swear by this bottle how thou camest hither. I
escaped upon a butt of sack which the sailors heaved
o'erboard, by this bottle, which I made of the bark of a
tree with mine own hands since I was cast ashore.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
[To STEPHANO] I’ll swear upon that bottle to be thy
true subject, for the liquor is not earthly.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text: [to TRINCULO] Here. Swear then how thou escapedst.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
Swum ashore, man, like a duck. I can swim like a duck,
I’ll be sworn.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
Here, kiss the book. Though thou canst swim like a
duck, thou art made like a goose.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text: O Stephano, hast any more of this?

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
The whole butt, man. My cellar is in a rock by th'
seaside where my wine is hid.—How now, mooncalf? How
does thine ague?

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text: Hast thou not dropped from heaven?

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
Out o' th' moon, I do assure thee. I was the man i’ the
moon when time was.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
I have seen thee in her and I do adore thee. My
mistress showed me thee and thy dog and thy bush.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
Come, swear to that, kiss the book. I will furnish it
anon with new contents, swear.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
By this good light, this is a very shallow monster. I
afeard of him! A very weak monster. The man i' th' moon!
A most poor credulous monster.— Well drawn, monster, in
good sooth!

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
[to STEPHANO] I’ll show thee every fertile inch o' th'
island.
And I will kiss thy foot. I prithee, be my god.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
By this light, a most perfidious and drunken monster.
When ’s god’s asleep, he’ll rob his bottle.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
[to STEPHANO] I’ll kiss thy foot. I’ll swear myself thy
subject.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text: Come on then. Down, and swear.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
I shall laugh myself to death at this puppy-headed
monster.
A most scurvy monster. I could find in my heart to beat
him—

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text: [To CALIBAN] Come, kiss.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
But that the poor monster’s in drink. An abominable
monster!

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
I’ll show thee the best springs. I’ll pluck thee
berries.
I’ll fish for thee and get thee wood enough.
A plague upon the tyrant that I serve!
I’ll bear him no more sticks, but follow thee,
Thou wondrous man.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
A most ridiculous monster, to make a wonder of a poor
drunkard.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
[to STEPHANO] I prithee, let me bring thee where crabs
grow.
And I with my long nails will dig thee pignuts,
Show thee a jay’s nest, and instruct thee how
To snare the nimble marmoset. I’ll bring thee
To clustering filberts, and sometimes I’ll get thee
Young scamels from the rock. Wilt thou go with me?

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
I prithee now, lead the way without any more talking.
Trinculo, the king and all our company else being
drowned, we will inherit here. Here, bear my bottle.
Fellow Trinculo, we’ll fill him by and by again.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
[sings drunkenly]
Farewell, master! Farewell, farewell.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text: A howling monster, a drunken monster.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
[sings]
No more dams I’ll make for fish,
Nor fetch in firing
At requiring,
Nor scrape trencher, nor wash dish.
'Ban, 'Ban, Ca-Caliban
Has a new master. Get a new man.
Freedom, high-day! High-day, freedom! Freedom,
high-day, freedom!

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text: O brave monster! Lead the way.
They exit.
FERDINAND enters, carrying a log.

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
There be some sports are painful, and their labor
Delight in them sets off. Some kinds of baseness
Are nobly undergone. And most poor matters
Point to rich ends. This my mean task
Would be as heavy to me as odious, but
The mistress which I serve quickens what’s dead
And makes my labors pleasures. Oh, she is
Ten times more gentle than her father’s crabbed,
And he’s composed of harshness. I must remove
Some thousands of these logs and pile them up,
Upon a sore injunction. My sweet mistress
Weeps when she sees me work, and says such baseness
Had never like executor. I forget,
But these sweet thoughts do even refresh my labors,
Most busiest when I do it.
MIRANDA enters, followed by PROSPERO who remains unseen by the others.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
Alas now, pray you,
Work not so hard. I would the lightning had
Burnt up those logs that you are enjoined to pile!
Pray, set it down and rest you. When this burns,
'Twill weep for having wearied you. My father
Is hard at study. Pray now, rest yourself.
He’s safe for these three hours.

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
O most dear mistress,
The sun will set before I shall discharge
What I must strive to do.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
If you’ll sit down,
I’ll bear your logs the while. Pray, give me that.
I’ll carry it to the pile.

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
No, precious creature.
I had rather crack my sinews, break my back,
Than you should such dishonor undergo
While I sit lazy by.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
It would become me
As well as it does you, and I should do it
With much more ease, for my good will is to it
And yours it is against.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
[aside] Poor worm, thou art infected!
This visitation shows it.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text: You look wearily.

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
No, noble mistress. 'Tis fresh morning with me
When you are by at night. I do beseech you—
Chiefly that I might set it in my prayers—
What is your name?

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
Miranda. O my father,
I have broke your hest to say so!

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
Admired Miranda!
Indeed the top of admiration, worth
What’s dearest to th' world! Full many a lady
I have eyed with best regard and many a time
Th' harmony of their tongues hath into bondage
Brought my too diligent ear. For several virtues
Have I liked several women. Never any
With so full soul but some defect in her
Did quarrel with the noblest grace she owed
And put it to the foil. But you, O you,
So perfect and so peerless, are created
Of every creature’s best.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
I do not know
One of my sex, no woman’s face remember—
Save, from my glass, mine own. Nor have I seen
More that I may call men than you, good friend,
And my dear father. How features are abroad
I am skill-less of, but, by my modesty,
The jewel in my dower, I would not wish
Any companion in the world but you,
Nor can imagination form a shape
Besides yourself to like of. But I prattle
Something too wildly, and my father’s precepts
I therein do forget.

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
I am in my condition
A prince, Miranda—I do think, a king;
I would, not so!—and would no more endure
This wooden slavery than to suffer
The flesh-fly blow my mouth. Hear my soul speak.
The very instant that I saw you did
My heart fly to your service, there resides
To make me slave to it, and for your sake
Am I this patient log-man.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text: Do you love me?

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
O heaven, O earth, bear witness to this sound
And crown what I profess with kind event
If I speak true! If hollowly, invert
What best is boded me to mischief! I
Beyond all limit of what else i' th' world
Do love, prize, honor you.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
I am a fool
To weep at what I am glad of.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
[aside] Fair encounter
Of two most rare affections! Heavens rain grace
On that which breeds between 'em!

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text: Wherefore weep you?

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
At mine unworthiness, that dare not offer
What I desire to give, and much less take
What I shall die to want. But this is trifling,
And all the more it seeks to hide itself
The bigger bulk it shows. Hence, bashful cunning,
And prompt me, plain and holy innocence!
I am your wife if you will marry me.
If not, I’ll die your maid. To be your fellow
You may deny me, but I’ll be your servant
Whether you will or no.

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text: My mistress, dearest, and I thus humble ever.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text: My husband, then?

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
Ay, with a heart as willing
As bondage e'er of freedom. Here’s my hand.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
And mine, with my heart in ’t. And now farewell
Till half an hour hence.

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text: A thousand thousand!
MIRANDA and FERDINAND exit, in opposite directions.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
So glad of this as they I cannot be,
Who are surprised withal. But my rejoicing
At nothing can be more. I’ll to my book,
For yet ere supper-time must I perform
Much business appertaining.
He exits.
CALIBAN, STEPHANO, and TRINCULO enter.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
Tell not me. When the butt is out, we will drink water.
Not a drop before. Therefore bear up and board
'em.—Servant- monster, drink to me.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
“Servant-monster?” The folly of this island. They say
there’s but five upon this isle. We are three of them.
If th' other two be brained like us, the state totters.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
Drink, servant-monster, when I bid thee. Thy eyes are
almost set in thy head.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
Where should they be set else? He were a brave monster
indeed, if they were set in his tail.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
My man-monster hath drowned his tongue in sack. For my
part, the sea cannot drown me. I swam, ere I could
recover the shore, five and thirty leagues off and on.
By this light, thou shalt be my lieutenant, monster, or
my standard.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text: Your lieutenant, if you list. He’s no standard.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text: We’ll not run, Monsieur Monster.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
Nor go neither. But you’ll lie like dogs, and yet say
nothing neither.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
Mooncalf, speak once in thy life, if thou beest a good
mooncalf.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
How does thy honor? Let me lick thy shoe.
[indicates TRINCULO] I’ll not serve him. He’s not
valiant.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
[to CALIBAN] Thou liest, most ignorant monster. I am in
case to justle a constable. Why, thou deboshed fish,
thou, was there ever man a coward that hath drunk so
much sack as I today? Wilt thou tell a monstrous lie,
being but half a fish and half a monster?

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
[to STEPHANO]
Lo, how he mocks me! Wilt thou let him, my lord?

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
“Lord,” quoth he? That a monster should be such a
natural!

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
[to STEPHANO]
Lo, lo, again! Bite him to death, I prithee.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
Trinculo, keep a good tongue in your head. If you prove
a mutineer, the next tree. The poor monster’s my
subject and he shall not suffer indignity.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
I thank my noble lord. Wilt thou be pleased to hearken
once again to the suit I made to thee?

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
Marry, will I. Kneel and repeat it. I will stand, and
so shall
Trinculo.
ARIEL enters, invisible.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
[kneeling] As I told thee before, I am subject to a
tyrant, a sorcerer that by his cunning hath cheated me
of the island.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: Thou liest.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
[to TRINCULO] Thou liest, thou jesting monkey, thou! I
would my valiant master would destroy thee. I do not
lie.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
Trinculo, if you trouble him any more in ’s tale, by
this hand, I will supplant some of your teeth.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text: Why, I said nothing.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text: Mum, then, and no more. Proceed.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
I say, by sorcery he got this isle.
From me he got it. If thy greatness will
Revenge it on him—for I know thou darest,
But this thing dare not—

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text: That’s most certain.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text: Thou shalt be lord of it and I’ll serve thee.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
How now shall this be compassed?
Canst thou bring me to th' party?

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
Yea, yea, my lord. I’ll yield him thee asleep,
Where thou mayst knock a nail into his head.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: Thou liest. Thou canst not.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
What a pied ninny’s this! Thou scurvy patch!
I do beseech thy greatness, give him blows
And take his bottle from him. When that’s gone,
He shall drink naught but brine, for I’ll not show him
Where the quick freshes are.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
Trinculo, run into no further danger. Interrupt the
monster one word further, and, by this hand, I’ll turn
my mercy out o' doors and make a stockfish of thee.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text: Why, what did I? I did nothing. I’ll go farther off.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text: Didst thou not say he lied?

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: Thou liest.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
[to TRINCULO] Do I so? Take thou that.
[beatsTRINCULO]
As you like this, give me the lie another time.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
I did not give the lie. Out o' your wits and hearing
too? A pox o' your bottle! This can sack and drinking
do. A murrain on your monster, and the devil take your
fingers!

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text: Ha, ha, ha!

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
Now, forward with your tale.—Prithee, stand farther
off.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
Beat him enough. After a little time,
I’ll beat him too.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text: Stand farther.—Come, proceed.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
Why, as I told thee, ’tis a custom with him,
I' th' afternoon to sleep. There thou mayst brain him,
Having first seized his books; or with a log
Batter his skull; or paunch him with a stake;
Or cut his weasand with thy knife. Remember
First to possess his books, for without them
He’s but a sot, as I am, nor hath not
One spirit to command. They all do hate him
As rootedly as I. Burn but his books.
He has brave utensils—for so he calls them—
Which when he has a house, he’ll deck withal.
And that most deeply to consider is
The beauty of his daughter. He himself
Calls her a nonpareil. I never saw a woman,
But only Sycorax my dam and she.
But she as far surpasseth Sycorax
As great’st does least.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text: Is it so brave a lass?

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
Ay, lord. She will become thy bed, I warrant.
And bring thee forth brave brood.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
Monster, I will kill this man. His daughter and I will
be king and queen—save our graces!—and Trinculo and
thyself shall be viceroys. Dost thou like the plot,
Trinculo?

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text: Excellent.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
Give me thy hand. I am sorry I beat thee. But while
thou livest, keep a good tongue in thy head.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
Within this half hour will he be asleep. Wilt thou
destroy him then?

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text: Ay, on mine honor.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: [aside] This will I tell my master.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
Thou makest me merry. I am full of pleasure.
Let us be jocund. Will you troll the catch
You taught me but whilere?

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
At thy request, monster, I will do reason, any reason.
Come on, Trinculo, let us sing.
[sings]
Flout 'em and scout 'em,
And scout 'em and flout 'em.
Thought is free.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text: That’s not the tune.
ARIEL plays the tune on a small drum and a flute.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text: What is this same?

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
This is the tune of our catch, played by the picture of
Nobody.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
If thou beest a man, show thyself in thy likeness. If
thou beest a devil, take ’t as thou list.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text: O, forgive me my sins!

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
He that dies pays all debts.—I defy thee!—Mercy upon
us!

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text: Art thou afeard?

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text: No, monster, not I.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
Be not afeard. The isle is full of noises,
Sounds, and sweet airs that give delight and hurt not.
Sometimes a thousand twangling instruments
Will hum about mine ears, and sometime voices
That, if I then had waked after long sleep,
Will make me sleep again. And then, in dreaming,
The clouds methought would open and show riches
Ready to drop upon me, that when I waked
I cried to dream again.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
This will prove a brave kingdom to me, where I shall
have my music for nothing.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text: When Prospero is destroyed.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text: That shall be by and by. I remember the story.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
The sound is going away. Let’s follow it, and after do
our work.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
Lead, monster; we’ll follow. I would I could see this
taborer. He lays it on.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text: Wilt come? I’ll follow, Stephano.
They all exit.
ALONSO, SEBASTIAN, ANTONIO, GONZALO, ADRIAN, FRANCISCO, and others enter.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
[to ALONSO] By 'r lakin, I can go no further, sir.
My old bones ache. Here’s a maze trod indeed
Through forthrights and meanders. By your patience,
I needs must rest me.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text:
Old lord, I cannot blame thee,
Who am myself attached with weariness
To th' dulling of my spirits. Sit down and rest.
Even here I will put off my hope and keep it
No longer for my flatterer. He is drowned
Whom thus we stray to find, and the sea mocks
Our frustrate search on land. Well, let him go.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
[aside to SEBASTIAN] I am right glad that he’s so out
of hope.
Do not, for one repulse, forego the purpose
That you resolved t' effect.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
[aside to ANTONIO] The next advantage
Will we take throughly.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
[aside to SEBASTIAN] Let it be tonight,
For now they are oppressed with travel. They
Will not, nor cannot, use such vigilance
As when they are fresh.
Solemn and strange music plays.
PROSPERO enters above the stage, invisible.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: [aside to ANTONIO] I say, tonight. No more.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text: What harmony is this? My good friends, hark!

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text: Marvelous sweet music!

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text: Give us kind keepers, heavens! What were these?

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
A living drollery. Now I will believe
That there are unicorns, that in Arabia
There is one tree, the phoenix' throne, one phoenix
At this hour reigning there.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
I’ll believe both
And what does else want credit, come to me,
And I’ll be sworn ’tis true. Travelers ne'er did lie,
Though fools at home condemn 'em.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
If in Naples
I should report this now, would they believe me?
If I should say, I saw such islanders—
For, certes, these are people of the island—
Who, though they are of monstrous shape, yet note,
Their manners are more gentle-kind than of
Our human generation you shall find
Many—nay, almost any.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
[aside] Honest lord,
Thou hast said well, for some of you there present
Are worse than devils.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text:
I cannot too much muse
Such shapes, such gesture, and such sound, expressing,
Although they want the use of tongue, a kind
Of excellent dumb discourse.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text: [aside] Praise in departing.

units.speaker: FRANCISCO
units.text: They vanished strangely.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
No matter, since
They have left their viands behind, for we have
stomachs.
Will ’t please you taste of what is here?

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text: Not I.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
Faith, sir, you need not fear. When we were boys,
Who would believe that there were mountaineers
Dewlapped like bulls, whose throats had hanging at 'em
Wallets of flesh, or that there were such men
Whose heads stood in their breasts?—which now we find
Each putter-out of five for one will bring us
Good warrant of.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text:
I will stand to and feed,
Although my last. No matter, since I feel
The best is past. Brother, my lord the duke,
Stand to and do as we.
ARIEL enters in the form of a harpy. ARIEL flaps his wings on the table, and by means of some kind of device on stage, the food disappears from the table.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text:
[to ALONSO, ANTONIO, and SEBASTIAN]
You are three men of sin, whom Destiny,
That hath to instrument this lower world
And what is in ’t, the never-surfeited sea
Hath caused to belch up you— and on this island
Where man doth not inhabit, you ’mongst men
Being most unfit to live. I have made you mad,
And even with suchlike valor men hang and drown
Their proper selves.
[ALONSO, SEBASTIAN, and ANTONIO draw their swords]
You fools, I and my fellows
Are ministers of fate. The elements
Of whom your swords are tempered may as well
Wound the loud winds or with bemocked-at stabs
Kill the still-closing waters as diminish
One dowl that’s in my plume. My fellow ministers
Are like invulnerable. If you could hurt,
Your swords are now too massy for your strengths
And will not be uplifted. But remember—
For that’s my business to you—that you three
From Milan did supplant good Prospero,
Exposed unto the sea, which hath requit it,
Him and his innocent child. For which foul deed
The powers—delaying, not forgetting—have
Incensed the seas and shores, yea, all the creatures,
Against your peace. Thee of thy son, Alonso,
They have bereft, and do pronounce by me
Lingering perdition, worse than any death
Can be at once, shall step by step attend
You and your ways; whose wraths to guard you from—
Which here, in this most desolate isle, else falls
Upon your heads—is nothing but hearts' sorrow
And a clear life ensuing.
