As quiet music plays, the shapes enter again and dance, making mocking gestures and nasty faces, they carry out the banquet table.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
[aside] Bravely the figure of this harpy hast thou
Performed, my Ariel. A grace it had, devouring.
Of my instruction hast thou nothing bated
In what thou hadst to say. So with good life
And observation strange, my meaner ministers
Their several kinds have done. My high charms work
And these mine enemies are all knit up
In their distractions. They now are in my power,
And in these fits I leave them while I visit
Young Ferdinand, whom they suppose is drowned,
And his and mine loved darling.
PROSPERO exits from his place above the stage.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
[to ALONSO] I' th' name of something holy, sir, why
stand you
In this strange stare?

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text:
Oh, it is monstrous, monstrous.
Methought the billows spoke and told me of it,
The winds did sing it to me, and the thunder,
That deep and dreadful organ pipe, pronounced
The name of Prosper. It did bass my trespass.
Therefore my son i' th' ooze is bedded, and
I’ll seek him deeper than e'er plummet sounded
And with him there lie mudded.
ALONSO exits.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
But one fiend at a time,
I’ll fight their legions o'er.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: I’ll be thy second.
SEBASTIAN and ANTONIO exit.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
All three of them are desperate. Their great guilt,
Like poison given to work a great time after,
Now 'gins to bite the spirits. I do beseech you
That are of suppler joints, follow them swiftly
And hinder them from what this ecstasy
May now provoke them to.

units.speaker: ADRIAN
units.text: Follow, I pray you.
They all exit.
PROSPERO, FERDINAND, and MIRANDA enter.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
[to FERDINAND] If I have too austerely punished you,
Your compensation makes amends, for I
Have given you here a third of mine own life—
Or that for which I live— who once again
I tender to thy hand. All thy vexations
Were but my trials of thy love and thou
Hast strangely stood the test. Here, afore heaven,
I ratify this my rich gift. O Ferdinand,
Do not smile at me that I boast of her,
For thou shalt find she will outstrip all praise
And make it halt behind her.

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
I do believe it
Against an oracle.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Then as my gift and thine own acquisition
Worthily purchased, take my daughter. But
If thou dost break her virgin knot before
All sanctimonious ceremonies may
With full and holy rite be ministered,
No sweet aspersion shall the heavens let fall
To make this contract grow, but barren hate,
Sour-eyed disdain, and discord shall bestrew
The union of your bed with weeds so loathly
That you shall hate it both. Therefore take heed,
As Hymen’s lamps shall light you.

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
As I hope
For quiet days, fair issue, and long life,
With such love as ’tis now, the murkiest den,
The most opportune place, the strong’st suggestion,
Our worser genius can shall never melt
Mine honor into lust to take away
The edge of that day’s celebration
When I shall think, or Phoebus' steeds are foundered,
Or night kept chained below.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Fairly spoke.
Sit then and talk with her. She is thine own.
What, Ariel! My industrious servant, Ariel!
ARIEL enters.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: What would my potent master? Here I am.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Thou and thy meaner fellows your last service
Did worthily perform, and I must use you
In such another trick. Go bring the rabble,
O'er whom I give thee power, here to this place.
Incite them to quick motion, for I must
Bestow upon the eyes of this young couple
Some vanity of mine art. It is my promise,
And they expect it from me.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: Presently?

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text: Ay, with a twink.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text:
Before you can say “Come” and “Go,”
And breathe twice and cry “So, so!”
Each one, tripping on his toe,
Will be here with mop and mow.
Do you love me, master, no?

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Dearly my delicate Ariel. Do not approach
Till thou dost hear me call.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: Well, I conceive.
ARIEL exits.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
[to FERDINAND] Look thou be true. Do not give dalliance
Too much the rein. The strongest oaths are straw
To th' fire i' th' blood. Be more abstemious,
Or else, goodnight your vow.

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
I warrant you, sir,
The white cold virgin snow upon my heart
Abates the ardor of my liver.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Well.
Now come, my Ariel! Bring a corollary,
Rather than want a spirit. Appear and pertly!—
No tongue. All eyes! Be silent.
Soft music plays.
IRIS enters.

units.speaker: IRIS
units.text:
Ceres, most bounteous lady, thy rich leas
Of wheat, rye, barley, vetches, oats, and peas;
Thy turfy mountains, where live nibbling sheep,
And flat meads thatched with stover, them to keep;
Thy banks with pionèd and twillèd brims,
Which spongy April at thy hest betrims
To make cold nymphs chaste crowns; and thy broom
groves,
Whose shadow the dismissèd bachelor loves,
Being lass-lorn; thy pole-clipped vineyard;
And thy sea-marge, sterile and rocky hard,
Where thou thyself dost air— the Queen o' th' Sky,
Whose watery arch and messenger am I,
Bids thee leave these, and with her sovereign grace,
Here on this grass plot, in this very place,
To come and sport. Her peacocks fly amain.

units.speaker: IRIS
units.text: Approach, rich Ceres, her to entertain.
CERES enters.

units.speaker: CERES
units.text:
Hail, many-colored messenger, that ne'er
Dost disobey the wife of Jupiter;
Who with thy saffron wings upon my flowers
Diffusest honey drops, refreshing showers;
And with each end of thy blue bow dost crown
My bosky acres and my unshrubbed down,
Rich scarf to my proud earth. Why hath thy queen
Summoned me hither to this short-grassed green?

units.speaker: IRIS
units.text:
A contract of true love to celebrate,
And some donation freely to estate
On the blessed lovers.

units.speaker: CERES
units.text:
Tell me, heavenly bow,
If Venus or her son, as thou dost know,
Do now attend the queen? Since they did plot
The means that dusky Dis my daughter got,
Her and her blind boy’s scandaled company
I have forsworn.

units.speaker: IRIS
units.text:
Of her society
Be not afraid. I met her deity
Cutting the clouds towards Paphos, and her son
Dove-drawn with her. Here thought they to have done
Some wanton charm upon this man and maid,
Whose vows are that no bed-right shall be paid
Till Hymen’s torch be lighted—but in vain.
Mars’s hot minion is returned again.
Her waspish-headed son has broke his arrows,
Swears he will shoot no more, but play with sparrows
And be a boy right out.

units.speaker: CERES
units.text:
Highest queen of state,
Great Juno, comes. I know her by her gait.

units.speaker: JUNO
units.text:
How does my bounteous sister? Go with me
To bless this twain that they may prosperous be,
And honored in their issue.
They sing.

units.speaker: JUNO
units.text:
Honor, riches, marriage, blessing,
Long continuance, and increasing,
Hourly joys be still upon you.
Juno sings her blessings on you.

units.speaker: CERES
units.text:
Earth’s increase, foison plenty,
Barns and garners never empty,
Vines and clustering bunches growing,
Plants with goodly burden bowing—
Spring come to you at the farthest
In the very end of harvest.
Scarcity and want shall shun you.
Ceres' blessing so is on you.

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
This is a most majestic vision, and
Harmonious charmingly. May I be bold
To think these spirits?

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Spirits, which by mine art
I have from their confines called to enact
My present fancies.

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
Let me live here ever.
So rare a wondered father and a wife
Makes this place paradise.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Sweet now, silence.
Juno and Ceres whisper seriously.
There’s something else to do. Hush and be mute,
Or else our spell is marred.

units.speaker: IRIS
units.text:
You nymphs, called Naiads of the windring brooks,
With your sedged crowns and ever-harmless looks,
Leave your crisp channels and on this green land
Answer your summons, Juno does command.
Come, temperate nymphs, and help to celebrate
A contract of true love. Be not too late.
Several NYMPHS enter.

units.speaker: IRIS
units.text:
You
sunburnt sicklemen of August weary,
Come hither from the furrow and be merry.
Make holiday. Your rye-straw hats put on,
And these fresh nymphs encounter every one
In country footing.
Several farmers enter, dressed appropriately. They join the nymphs in a graceful dance. At the end of the dance PROSPERO suddenly is startled and speaks.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
I had forgot that foul conspiracy
Of the beast Caliban and his confederates
Against my life. The minute of their plot
Is almost come.— Well done. Avoid, no more!

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
[to MIRANDA] This is strange. Your father’s in some
passion
That works him strongly.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
Never till this day
Saw I him touched with anger so distempered.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
[to FERDINAND] You do look, my son, in a moved sort,
As if you were dismayed. Be cheerful, sir.
Our revels now are ended. These our actors,
As I foretold you, were all spirits and
Are melted into air, into thin air.
And like the baseless fabric of this vision,
The cloud-capped towers, the gorgeous palaces,
The solemn temples, the great globe itself,
Ye all which it inherit, shall dissolve,
And like this insubstantial pageant faded,
Leave not a rack behind. We are such stuff
As dreams are made on, and our little life
Is rounded with a sleep. Sir, I am vexed.
Bear with my weakness. My old brain is troubled.
Be not disturbed with my infirmity.
If you be pleased, retire into my cell
And there repose. A turn or two I’ll walk
To still my beating mind.

units.speaker: FERDINAND MIRANDA
units.text: We wish your peace.
FERDINAND and MIRANDA exit.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text: Come with a thought. I thank thee, Ariel. Come.
ARIEL enters.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: Thy thoughts I cleave to. What’s thy pleasure?

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Spirit,
We must prepare to meet with Caliban.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text:
Ay, my commander. When I presented Ceres,
I thought to have told thee of it, but I feared
Lest I might anger thee.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text: Say again, where didst thou leave these varlets?

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text:
I told you, sir, they were red-hot with drinking,
So full of valor that they smote the air
For breathing in their faces, beat the ground
For kissing of their feet; yet always bending
Towards their project. Then I beat my tabor,
At which, like unbacked colts, they pricked their ears,
Advanced their eyelids, lifted up their noses
As they smelt music. So I charmed their ears
That, calflike, they my lowing followed through
Toothed briers, sharp furzes, pricking gorse, and
thorns,
Which entered their frail shins. At last I left them
I' th' filthy-mantled pool beyond your cell,
There dancing up to th' chins, that the foul lake
O'erstunk their feet.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
This was well done, my bird.
Thy shape invisible retain thou still.
The trumpery in my house, go bring it hither
For stale to catch these thieves.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: I go, I go.
ARIEL exits.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
A devil, a born devil on whose nature
Nurture can never stick, on whom my pains,
Humanely taken, all, all lost, quite lost.
And as with age his body uglier grows,
So his mind cankers. I will plague them all,
Even to roaring.
ARIEL enters, carrying sparkling clothes.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text: Come, hang them on this line.
CALIBAN, STEPHANO, and TRINCULO enter. They all are wet.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
Pray you, tread softly, that the blind mole may not
hear a foot fall. We now are near his cell.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
Monster, your fairy, which you say is a harmless fairy,
has done little better than played the jack with us.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
Monster, I do smell all horse piss, at which my nose is
in great indignation.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
So is mine. Do you hear, monster? If I should take a
displeasure against you, look you—

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text: Thou wert but a lost monster.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
Good my lord, give me thy favor still.
Be patient, for the prize I’ll bring thee to
Shall hoodwink this mischance. Therefore speak softly.
All’s hushed as midnight yet.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text: Ay, but to lose our bottles in the pool—

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
There is not only disgrace and dishonor in that,
monster, but an infinite loss.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
That’s more to me than my wetting. Yet this is your
harmless fairy, monster.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
I will fetch off my bottle, though I be o'er ears for
my labor.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
Prithee, my king, be quiet. Seest thou here,
This is the mouth o' th' cell. No noise, and enter.
Do that good mischief which may make this island
Thine own for ever, and I, thy Caliban,
For aye thy foot-licker.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text: Give me thy hand. I do begin to have bloody thoughts.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
[seeing the apparel]
O King Stephano! O peer, O worthy Stephano, look what a
wardrobe here is for thee!

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text: Let it alone, thou fool. It is but trash.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
Oh, ho, monster, we know what belongs to a frippery.—
[puts on a gown] O King Stephano!

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
Put off that gown, Trinculo. By this hand, I’ll have
that gown.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text: Thy grace shall have it.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
The dropsy drown this fool! What do you mean
To dote thus on such luggage? Let’s alone,
And do the murder first. If he awake,
From toe to crown he’ll fill our skins with pinches,
Make us strange stuff.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
Be you quiet, monster.—Mistress line, is not this my
jerkin? Now is the jerkin under the line.—Now, jerkin,
you are like to lose your hair and prove a bald jerkin.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
Do, do. We steal by line and level, an ’t like your
grace.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
I thank thee for that jest. Here’s a garment for ’t.
Wit shall not go unrewarded while I am king of this
country. “Steal by line and level” is an excellent pass
of pate. There’s another garment for ’t.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
Monster, come, put some lime upon your fingers, and
away with the rest.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
I will have none on ’t. We shall lose our time,
And all be turned to barnacles or to apes
With foreheads villainous low.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
Monster, lay to your fingers. Help to bear this away
where my hogshead of wine is, or I’ll turn you out of my
kingdom. Go to, carry this.

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text: And this.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text: Ay, and this.
The sound of hunters comes from offstage. Various spirits enter in the form of hunting dogs, which chase STEPHANO, TRINCULO, and CALIBAN around. PROSPERO and ARIEL enter and urge the dogs on.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text: Hey, Mountain, hey!

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: Silver. There it goes, Silver!

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text: Fury, Fury! There, Tyrant, there. Hark, hark!
The spirits chase CALIBAN, STEPHANO, and TRINCULO offstage.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Go charge my goblins that they grind their joints
With dry convulsions, shorten up their sinews
With agèd cramps, and more pinch-spotted make them
Than pard or cat o' mountain.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: Hark, they roar.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Let them be hunted soundly. At this hour
Lie at my mercy all mine enemies.
Shortly shall all my labors end, and thou
Shalt have the air at freedom. For a little
Follow, and do me service.
They exit.
PROSPERO enters wearing his magic robes, with ARIEL.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Now does my project gather to a head.
My charms crack not, my spirits obey, and time
Goes upright with his carriage. How’s the day?

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text:
On the sixth hour, at which time, my lord,
You said our work should cease.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
I did say so
When first I raised the tempest. Say, my spirit,
How fares the king and ’s followers?

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text:
Confined together
In the same fashion as you gave in charge,
Just as you left them, all prisoners, sir,
In the line grove which weather-fends your cell.
They cannot budge till your release. The king,
His brother, and yours, abide all three distracted,
And the remainder mourning over them,
Brimful of sorrow and dismay. But chiefly
Him that you termed, sir, “the good old Lord Gonzalo,”
His tears run down his beard like winter’s drops
From eaves of reeds. Your charm so strongly works 'em
That if you now beheld them, your affections
Would become tender.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text: Dost thou think so, spirit?

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: Mine would, sir, were I human.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
And mine shall.
Hast thou, which art but air, a touch, a feeling
Of their afflictions, and shall not myself,
One of their kind, that relish all as sharply
Passion as they, be kindlier moved than thou art?
Though with their high wrongs I am struck to th' quick,
Yet with my nobler reason 'gainst my fury
Do I take part. The rarer action is
In virtue than in vengeance. They being penitent,
The sole drift of my purpose doth extend
Not a frown further. Go release them, Ariel.
My charms I’ll break, their senses I’ll restore,
And they shall be themselves.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: I’ll fetch them, sir.
ARIEL exits.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
[tracing a circle on the ground]
Ye elves of hills, brooks, standing lakes, and groves,
And ye that on the sands with printless foot
Do chase the ebbing Neptune and do fly him
When he comes back; you demi-puppets that
By moonshine do the green sour ringlets make,
Whereof the ewe not bites; and you whose pastime
Is to make midnight mushrooms, that rejoice
To hear the solemn curfew; by whose aid,
Weak masters though ye be, I have bedimmed
The noontide sun, called forth the mutinous winds,
And ’twixt the green sea and the azured vault
Set roaring war—to th' dread rattling thunder
Have I given fire, and rifted Jove’s stout oak
With his own bolt; the strong-based promontory
Have I made shake, and by the spurs plucked up
The pine and cedar; graves at my command
Have waked their sleepers, oped, and let 'em forth
By my so potent art. But this rough magic
I here abjure, and when I have required
Some heavenly music, which even now I do,
To work mine end upon their senses that
This airy charm is for, I’ll break my staff,
Bury it certain fathoms in the earth,
And deeper than did ever plummet sound
I’ll drown my book.
Solemn music plays.

ARIEL enters, followed by ALONSO who is acting as if he is crazy and is tended to by GONZALO. SEBASTIAN and ANTONIO enter, also seeming crazy, and are tended to by ADRIAN and FRANCISCO. They all enter the circle that PROSPERO has drawn and stand still, under a spell. PROSPERO watches them, and then speaks.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
A solemn air and the best comforter
To an unsettled fancy cure thy brains,
Now useless, boiled within thy skull. There stand,
For you are spell-stopped.
Holy Gonzalo, honorable man,
Mine eyes, ev'n sociable to the show of thine,
Fall fellowly drops. The charm dissolves apace,
And as the morning steals upon the night,
Melting the darkness, so their rising senses
Begin to chase the ignorant fumes that mantle
Their clearer reason. O good Gonzalo,
My true preserver and a loyal sir
To him you follow’st, I will pay thy graces
Home both in word and deed. Most cruelly
Didst thou, Alonso, use me and my daughter.
Thy brother was a furtherer in the act.
Thou art pinched for ’t now, Sebastian. Flesh and
blood,
You brother mine, that entertained ambition,
Expelled remorse and nature, whom, with Sebastian,
Whose inward pinches therefore are most strong,
Would here have killed your king—I do forgive thee,
Unnatural though thou art. Their understanding
Begins to swell, and the approaching tide
Will shortly fill the reasonable shore
That now lies foul and muddy. Not one of them
That yet looks on me, or would know me. Ariel,
Fetch me the hat and rapier in my cell.
I will discase me, and myself present
As I was sometime Milan. Quickly, spirit.
Thou shalt ere long be free.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text:
[sings and helps to attire PROSPERO]
Where the bee sucks, there suck I.
In a cowslip’s bell I lie.
There I couch when owls do cry.
On the bat’s back I do fly
After summer merrily.
Merrily, merrily shall I live now
Under the blossom that hangs on the bough.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Why, that’s my dainty Ariel. I shall miss thee,
But yet thou shalt have freedom.—So, so, so.—
To the king’s ship, invisible as thou art.
There shalt thou find the mariners asleep
Under the hatches. The Master and the Boatswain
Being awake, enforce them to this place,
And presently, I prithee.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text:
I drink the air before me, and return
Or ere your pulse twice beat.
ARIEL exits.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
All torment, trouble, wonder, and amazement
Inhabits here. Some heavenly power guide us
Out of this fearful country!

[to ALONSO] Behold, sir King,
The wrongèd Duke of Milan, Prospero.
For more assurance that a living prince
Does now speak to thee, I embrace thy body.
And to thee and thy company I bid
A hearty welcome. [embraces ALONSO]

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text:
Whe'er thou beest he or no,
Or some enchanted trifle to abuse me,
As late I have been, I not know. Thy pulse
Beats as of flesh and blood. And since I saw thee,
Th' affliction of my mind amends, with which
I fear a madness held me. This must crave—
An if this be at all—a most strange story.
Thy dukedom I resign and do entreat
Thou pardon me my wrongs. But how should Prospero
Be living and be here?

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
[to GONZALO] First, noble friend,
Let me embrace thine age, whose honor cannot
Be measured or confined.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
Whether this be
Or be not, I’ll not swear.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
You do yet taste
Some subtleties o' th' isle, that will not let you
Believe things certain. Welcome, my friends all.
[aside to SEBASTIAN and ANTONIO]
But you, my brace of lords, were I so minded,
I here could pluck his highness' frown upon you
And justify you traitors. At this time
I will tell no tales.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: The devil speaks in him.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
No.— [to ANTONIO]
For you, most wicked sir, whom to call brother
Would even infect my mouth, I do forgive
Thy rankest fault, all of them, a nd require
My dukedom of thee, which perforce, I know,
Thou must restore.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text:
If thou beest Prospero,
Give us particulars of thy preservation,
How thou hast met us here, whom three hours since
Were wracked upon this shore, where I have lost—
How sharp the point of this remembrance is!
My dear son Ferdinand.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text: I am woe for ’t, sir.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text:
Irreparable is the loss, and patience
Says it is past her cure.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
I rather think
You have not sought her help, of whose soft grace
For the like loss I have her sovereign aid,
And rest myself content.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text: You the like loss?

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
As great to me as late. And, supportable
To make the dear loss, have I means much weaker
Than you may call to comfort you, for I
Have lost my daughter.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text:
A daughter?
O heavens, that they were living both in Naples,
The king and queen there! That they were, I wish
Myself were mudded in that oozy bed
Where my son lies.—When did you lose your daughter?

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
In this last tempest. I perceive these lords
At this encounter do so much admire
That they devour their reason and scarce think
Their eyes do offices of truth, their words
Are natural breath. But howsoev'r you have
Been justled from your senses, know for certain
That I am Prospero and that very duke
Which was thrust forth of Milan, who most strangely
Upon this shore where you were wracked, was landed,
To be the lord on ’t. No more yet of this,
For ’tis a chronicle of day by day,
Not a relation for a breakfast, nor
Befitting this first meeting.
[to ALONSO] Welcome, sir.
This cell’s my court. Here have I few attendants
And subjects none abroad. Pray you, look in.
My dukedom since you have given me again,
I will requite you with as good a thing,
At least bring forth a wonder to content ye
As much as me my dukedom.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text: [to FERDINAND] Sweet lord, you play me false.

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
No, my dearest love,
I would not for the world.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
Yes, for a score of kingdoms you should wrangle,
And I would call it fair play.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text:
If this prove
A vision of the Island, one dear son
Shall I twice lose.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: A most high miracle!

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
[seeing ALONSO and kneeling]
Though the seas threaten, they are merciful.
I have cursed them without cause.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text:
Now all the blessings
Of a glad father, compass thee about.
Arise, and say how thou camest here.

units.speaker: MIRANDA
units.text:
Oh, wonder!
How many goodly creatures are there here!
How beauteous mankind is! O brave new world,
That has such people in ’t!

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text: 'Tis new to thee.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text:
[to FERDINAND]
What is this maid with whom thou wast at play?
Your eld’st acquaintance cannot be three hours.
Is she the goddess that hath severed us
And brought us thus together?

units.speaker: FERDINAND
units.text:
Sir, she is mortal.
But by immortal providence, she’s mine.
I chose her when I could not ask my father
For his advice, nor thought I had one. She
Is daughter to this famous Duke of Milan,
Of whom so often I have heard renown
But never saw before, of whom I have
Received a second life. And second father
This lady makes him to me.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text:
I am hers.
But oh, how oddly will it sound that I
Must ask my child forgiveness!

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
There, sir, stop.
Let us not burden our remembrances with
A heaviness that’s gone.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
I have inly wept,
Or should have spoke ere this. Look down, you gods,
And on this couple drop a blessèd crown,
For it is you that have chalked forth the way
Which brought us hither.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text: I say, amen, Gonzalo.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
Was Milan thrust from Milan, that his issue
Should become kings of Naples? O, rejoice
Beyond a common joy, and set it down
With gold on lasting pillars. In one voyage
Did Claribel her husband find at Tunis;
And Ferdinand, her brother, found a wife
Where he himself was lost; Prospero, his dukedom
In a poor isle; and all of us, ourselves
When no man was his own.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text:
[to FERDINAND and MIRANDA] Give me your hands.
Let grief and sorrow still embrace his heart
That doth not wish you joy.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text: Be it so. Amen.
ARIEL enters, with the MASTER and BOATSWAIN following behind, amazed.

units.speaker: GONZALO
units.text:
Oh, look, sir, look, sir! Here is more of us.
I prophesied if a gallows were on land,
This fellow could not drown.
[To BOATSWAIN] Now, blasphemy,
That swear’st grace o'erboard, not an oath on shore?
Hast thou no mouth by land? What is the news?

units.speaker: BOATSWAIN
units.text:
The best news is that we have safely found
Our king and company. The next, our ship—
Which, but three glasses since, we gave out split—
Is tight and yare and bravely rigged as when
We first put out to sea.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text:
[aside to PROSPERO] Sir, all this service
Have I done since I went.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text: [aside to ARIEL] My tricksy spirit!

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text:
These are not natural events. They strengthen
From strange to stranger.—
[to BOATSWAIN] Say, how came you hither?

units.speaker: BOATSWAIN
units.text:
If I did think, sir, I were well awake,
I’d strive to tell you. We were dead of sleep
And—how, we know not—all clapped under hatches,
Where but even now with strange and several noises
Of roaring, shrieking, howling, jingling chains,
And more diversity of sounds, all horrible,
We were awaked, straightway at liberty,
Where we, in all her trim, freshly beheld
Our royal, good, and gallant ship, our Master
Capering to eye her. On a trice, so please you,
Even in a dream were we divided from them
And were brought moping hither.

units.speaker: ARIEL
units.text: [aside to PROSPERO] Was ’t well done?

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
[aside to ARIEL] Bravely, my diligence. Thou shalt be
free.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text:
This is as strange a maze as e'er men trod,
And there is in this business more than nature
Was ever conduct of. Some oracle
Must rectify our knowledge.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Sir, my liege,
Do not infest your mind with beating on
The strangeness of this business. At picked leisure
Which shall be shortly, single I’ll resolve you—
Which to you shall seem probable—of every
These happened accidents. Till when, be cheerful
And think of each thing well.
[aside to ARIEL] Come hither, spirit.
Set Caliban and his companions free.
Untie the spell.
ARIEL exits.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
How
fares my gracious sir?
There are yet missing of your company
Some few odd lads that you remember not.
ARIEL enters, pushing in CALIBAN, STEPHANO, and TRINCULO wearing their stolen clothes.

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text:
Every man shift for all the rest and let no man take
care for himself, for all is but fortune. Coraggio,
bully-monster, coraggio!

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
If these be true spies which I wear in my head, here’s
a goodly sight.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
O Setebos, these be brave spirits indeed!
How fine my master is! I am afraid
He will chastise me.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
Ha, ha!
What things are these, my lord Antonio?
Will money buy 'em?

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
Very like. One of them
Is a plain fish, and no doubt marketable.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Mark but the badges of these men, my lords,
Then say if they be true.
[indicates CALIBAN] This misshapen knave,
His mother was a witch, and one so strong
That could control the moon, make flows and ebbs,
And deal in her command without her power.
These three have robbed me, and this demi-devil—
For he’s a bastard one—had plotted with them
To take my life. Two of these fellows you
Must know and own. This thing of darkness I
Acknowledge mine.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text: I shall be pinched to death.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text: Is not this Stephano, my drunken butler?

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: He is drunk now. Where had he wine?

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text:
And Trinculo is reeling ripe. Where should they
Find this grand liquor that hath gilded 'em?—
How camest thou in this pickle?

units.speaker: TRINCULO
units.text:
I have been in such a pickle since I saw you last that,
I fear me, will never out of my bones. I shall not fear
flyblowing.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: Why, how now, Stephano?

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text: O, touch me not. I am not Stephano, but a cramp.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text: You’d be king o' th' isle, sirrah?

units.speaker: STEPHANO
units.text: I should have been a sore one then.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text:
[indicating CALIBAN]
This is a strange thing as e'er I looked on.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
He is as disproportioned in his manners
As in his shape.— [to CALIBAN] Go, sirrah, to my cell.
Take with you your companions. As you look
To have my pardon, trim it handsomely.

units.speaker: CALIBAN
units.text:
Ay, that I will. And I’ll be wise hereafter
And seek for grace. What a thrice-double ass
Was I, to take this drunkard for a god
And worship this dull fool!

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text: Go to, away.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text:
[to STEPHANO and TRINCULO]
Hence, and bestow your luggage where you found it.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: Or stole it, rather.
CALIBAN, STEPHANO, and TRINCULO exit.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Sir, I invite your highness and your train
To my poor cell, where you shall take your rest
For this one night, which—part of it—I’ll waste
With such discourse as, I not doubt, shall make it
Go quick away: the story of my life
And the particular accidents gone by
Since I came to this isle. And in the morn
I’ll bring you to your ship and so to Naples,
Where I have hope to see the nuptial
Of these our dear-belovèd solemnized,
And thence retire me to my Milan, where
Every third thought shall be my grave.

units.speaker: ALONSO
units.text:
I long
To hear the story of your life, which must
Take the ear strangely.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
I’ll deliver all,
And promise you calm seas, auspicious gales,
And sail so expeditious that shall catch
Your royal fleet far off.— [aside to ARIEL] My Ariel,
chick,
That is thy charge. Then to the elements
Be free, and fare thou well!— Please you, draw near.
They all exit.
PROSPERO speaks.

units.speaker: PROSPERO
units.text:
Now my charms are all o'erthrown,
And what strength I have’s mine own,
Which is most faint. Now, ’tis true,
I must be here confined by you,
Or sent to Naples. Let me not,
Since I have my dukedom got
And pardoned the deceiver, dwell
In this bare island by your spell,
But release me from my bands
With the help of your good hands.
Gentle breath of yours my sails
Must fill, or else my project fails,
Which was to please. Now I want
Spirits to enforce, art to enchant,
And my ending is despair,
Unless I be relieved by prayer,
Which pierces so that it assaults
Mercy itself and frees all faults.
As you from crimes would pardoned be,
Let your indulgence set me free.
He exits.

sentences.text: Loud sounds of a storm with thunder and lightning. A ship’s MASTER and BOATSWAIN enter.
Loud sounds of a storm with thunder and lightning. A ship’s MASTER and BOATSWAIN enter.

sentences.speaker: MASTER
sentences.text: Boatswain!

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text: Here, master. What cheer?

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text: Here, master. What cheer?

sentences.speaker: MASTER
sentences.text:
Good, speak to th' mariners. Fall to ’t yarely, or we
run ourselves aground. Bestir, bestir.

sentences.speaker: MASTER
sentences.text:
Good, speak to th' mariners. Fall to ’t yarely, or we
run ourselves aground. Bestir, bestir.

sentences.speaker: MASTER
sentences.text:
Good, speak to th' mariners. Fall to ’t yarely, or we
run ourselves aground. Bestir, bestir.
The MASTER exits.
SAILORS enter.

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text:
Heigh, my hearts! Cheerly, cheerly, my hearts! Yare!
Yare!
Take in the topsail. Tend to th' master’s whistle.
Blow, till thou burst thy wind, if room enough!

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text:

Yare!
Take in the topsail. Tend to th' master’s whistle.
Blow, till thou burst thy wind, if room enough!

Yare!
Take in the topsail. Tend to th' master’s whistle.
Blow, till thou burst thy wind, if room enough!

Yare!
Take in the topsail. Tend to th' master’s whistle.

Yare!
Take in the topsail. Tend to th' master’s whistle.

Yare!
Take in the topsail. Tend to th' master’s whistle.
Blow, till thou burst thy wind, if room enough!

Heigh, my hearts! Cheerly, cheerly, my hearts! Yare!
Yare!
Take in the topsail. Tend to th' master’s whistle.
Blow, till thou burst thy wind, if room enough!

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text:
Heigh, my hearts! Cheerly, cheerly, my hearts! Yare!
Yare!
Take in the topsail. Tend to th' master’s whistle.
Blow, till thou burst thy wind, if room enough!
ALONSO, SEBASTIAN, ANTONIO, FERDINAND, GONZALO, and others enter.

sentences.speaker: ALONSO
sentences.text:
Good Boatswain, have care. Where’s the Master?
Play the men.

sentences.speaker: ALONSO
sentences.text:
Good Boatswain, have care. Where’s the Master?
Play the men.

sentences.speaker: ALONSO
sentences.text:
Good Boatswain, have care. Where’s the Master?
Play the men.

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text: I pray now, keep below.

sentences.speaker: ANTONIO
sentences.text: Where is the Master, Boatswain?

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text:
Do you not hear him? You mar our labor. Keep your
cabins.
You do assist the storm.

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text:
Do you not hear him? You mar our labor. Keep your
cabins.
You do assist the storm.

sentences.text:
Do you not hear him? You mar our labor. Keep your
cabins.
You do assist the storm.

You do assist the storm.

sentences.speaker: GONZALO
sentences.text: Nay, good, be patient.

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text:
When the sea is. Hence! What cares these roarers for
the name of king? To cabin, silence! Trouble us not.

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text:
When the sea is. Hence! What cares these roarers for
the name of king? To cabin, silence! Trouble us not.

sentences.text:

sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text:
When the sea is. Hence! What cares these roarers for
the name of king? To cabin, silence! Trouble us not.

sentences.speaker: GONZALO
sentences.text: Good, yet remember whom thou hast aboard.

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text:
None that I more love than myself. You are a councilor.
If you can command these elements to silence and work
the peace of the present, we will not hand a rope more.
Use your authority. If you cannot, give thanks you have
lived so long and make yourself ready in your cabin for
the mischance of the hour, if it so hap.— Cheerly, good
hearts!— Out of our way, I say.

sentences.speaker: GONZALO
sentences.text:
I have great comfort from this fellow. Methinks he hath
no drowning mark upon him. His complexion is perfect
gallows. Stand fast, good Fate, to his hanging. Make the
rope of his destiny our cable, for our own doth little
advantage. If he be not born to be hanged, our case is
miserable.

GONZALO exits with the other men of the royal court.
The BOATSWAIN enters.

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text:
Down with the topmast! Yare, lower, lower! Bring her to
try wi' th' main course.

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text:
Down with the topmast! Yare, lower, lower! Bring her to
try wi' th' main course.

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text:
Down with the topmast! Yare, lower, lower! Bring her to
try wi' th' main course.
A shout offstage.

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text:
A plague upon this howling! They are louder than the
weather or our office.

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text:
A plague upon this howling! They are louder than the
weather or our office.
SEBASTIAN, ANTONIO, and GONZALO enter.

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text:
Yet again?
What do you here? Shall we give o'er and drown?
Have you a mind to sink?

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text:
Yet again?
What do you here? Shall we give o'er and drown?
Have you a mind to sink?

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text:
Yet again?
What do you here? Shall we give o'er and drown?
Have you a mind to sink?

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text:
Yet again?
What do you here? Shall we give o'er and drown?
Have you a mind to sink?

sentences.speaker: SEBASTIAN
sentences.text:
A pox o' your throat, you bawling, blasphemous,
incharitable dog!

sentences.speaker: SEBASTIAN
sentences.text:
A pox o' your throat, you bawling, blasphemous,
incharitable dog!

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text: Work you, then.

sentences.speaker: ANTONIO
sentences.text:
Hang, cur! Hang, you whoreson insolent noisemaker! We
are less afraid to be drowned than thou art.

sentences.speaker: ANTONIO
sentences.text:
Hang, cur! Hang, you whoreson insolent noisemaker! We
are less afraid to be drowned than thou art.

sentences.speaker: ANTONIO
sentences.text:
Hang, cur! Hang, you whoreson insolent noisemaker! We
are less afraid to be drowned than thou art.

sentences.speaker: GONZALO
sentences.text:
I’ll warrant him for drowning though the ship were no
stronger than a nutshell and as leaky as an unstanched
wench.

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text:
Lay her a-hold, a-hold! Set her two courses off to sea
again.
Lay her off!

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text:
Lay her a-hold, a-hold! Set her two courses off to sea
again.
Lay her off!

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text:
Lay her a-hold, a-hold! Set her two courses off to sea
again.
Lay her off!
Wet SAILORS enter

sentences.speaker: MARINERS
sentences.text: All lost! To prayers, to prayers, all lost!

sentences.speaker: MARINERS
sentences.text: All lost! To prayers, to prayers, all lost!

sentences.speaker: MARINERS
sentences.text: All lost! To prayers, to prayers, all lost!
The SAILORS exit.

sentences.speaker: BOATSWAIN
sentences.text: What, must our mouths be cold?

sentences.speaker: GONZALO
sentences.text:
The king and prince at prayers. Let’s assist them, for
our case is as theirs.

sentences.speaker: GONZALO
sentences.text:
The king and prince at prayers. Let’s assist them, for
our case is as theirs.

sentences.speaker: SEBASTIAN
sentences.text: I’m out of patience.

sentences.speaker: ANTONIO
sentences.text:
We are merely cheated of our lives by drunkards. This
wide-chopped rascal—would thou mightst lie drowning the
washing of ten tides!

sentences.speaker: ANTONIO
sentences.text:
We are merely cheated of our lives by drunkards. This
wide-chopped rascal—would thou mightst lie drowning the
washing of ten tides!

sentences.speaker: GONZALO
sentences.text:
He’ll be hanged yet, though every drop of water swear
against it and gape at widest to glut him.

sentences.speaker: GONZALO
sentences.text:
He’ll be hanged yet, though every drop of water swear
against it and gape at widest to glut him.

against it and gape at widest to glut him.

sentences.speaker: VOICES
sentences.text:
[within] Mercy on us! We split, we split! Farewell, my
wife and children! Farewell, brother! We split, we
split, we split!

sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: ANTONIO
sentences.text: Let’s all sink wi' th' king.

sentences.speaker: SEBASTIAN
sentences.text: Let’s take leave of him.
ANTONIO and SEBASTIAN exit.

sentences.speaker: GONZALO
sentences.text:
Now would I give a thousand furlongs of sea for an acre
of barren ground: long heath, brown furze, anything.
The wills above be done, but I would fain die a dry
death.

sentences.speaker: GONZALO
sentences.text:
Now would I give a thousand furlongs of sea for an acre
of barren ground: long heath, brown furze, anything.
The wills above be done, but I would fain die a dry
death.
They exit.
PROSPERO and MIRANDA enter.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
If by your art, my dearest father, you have
Put the wild waters in this roar, allay them.
The sky, it seems, would pour down stinking pitch,
But that the sea, mounting to th' welkin’s cheek,
Dashes the fire out. Oh, I have suffered
With those that I saw suffer. A brave vessel
Who had, no doubt, some noble creature in her
Dashed all to pieces. Oh, the cry did knock
Against my very heart! Poor souls, they perished.
Had I been any god of power, I would
Have sunk the sea within the earth or ere
It should the good ship so have swallowed and
The fraughting souls within her.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Be collected.
No more amazement. Tell your piteous heart
There’s no harm done.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Be collected.
No more amazement. Tell your piteous heart
There’s no harm done.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Be collected.
No more amazement. Tell your piteous heart
There’s no harm done.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text: Oh, woe the day!

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
No harm.
I have done nothing but in care of thee,
Of thee, my dear one—thee my daughter, who
Art ignorant of what thou art, naught knowing
Of whence I am, nor that I am more better
Than Prospero, master of a full poor cell
And thy no greater father.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
More to know
Did never meddle with my thoughts.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
'Tis time
I should inform thee farther. Lend thy hand
And pluck my magic garment from me.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
'Tis time
I should inform thee farther. Lend thy hand
And pluck my magic garment from me.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
'Tis time
I should inform thee farther. Lend thy hand
And pluck my magic garment from me.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
'Tis time
I should inform thee farther. Lend thy hand
And pluck my magic garment from me.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Lie
there, my art. Wipe thou thine eyes. Have comfort.
The direful spectacle of the wrack, which touched
The very virtue of compassion in thee,
I have with such provision in mine art
So safely ordered that there is no soul—
No, not so much perdition as an hair
Betid to any creature in the vessel—
Which thou heard’st cry, which thou sawst sink. Sit
down.
For thou must now know farther.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
You have often
Begun to tell me what I am, but stopped
And left me to a bootless inquisition,
Concluding, “Stay. Not yet.”

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
The hour’s now come.
The very minute bids thee ope thine ear.
Obey and be attentive. Canst thou remember
A time before we came unto this cell?
I do not think thou canst, for then thou wast not
Out three years old.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
By what? By any other house or person?
Of anything the image tell me that
Hath kept with thy remembrance.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO

Of anything the image tell me that
Hath kept with thy remembrance.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
'Tis far off,
And rather like a dream than an assurance
That my remembrance warrants. Had I not
Four or five women once that tended me?

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
'Tis far off,
And rather like a dream than an assurance
That my remembrance warrants. Had I not
Four or five women once that tended me?

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Thou hadst, and more, Miranda. But how is it
That this lives in thy mind? What seest thou else
In the dark backward and abysm of time?
If thou rememberest aught ere thou camest here,
How thou camest here thou mayst.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text: But that I do not.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Twelve year since, Miranda, twelve year since,
Thy father was the Duke of Milan and
A prince of power.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text: Sir, are not you my father?

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Thy mother was a piece of virtue and
She said thou wast my daughter. And thy father
Was Duke of Milan, and thou his only heir
And princess no worse issued.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Thy mother was a piece of virtue and
She said thou wast my daughter. And thy father

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
Oh, the heavens!
What foul play had we that we came from thence?
Or blessèd was ’t we did?

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
Oh, the heavens!
What foul play had we that we came from thence?
Or blessèd was ’t we did?

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
Oh, the heavens!
What foul play had we that we came from thence?
Or blessèd was ’t we did?

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Both, both, my girl.
By foul play, as thou sayst, were we heaved thence,
But blessedly holp hither.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
Oh, my heart bleeds
To think o' th' teen that I have turned you to,
Which is from my remembrance! Please you, farther.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
Oh, my heart bleeds

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
My brother and thy uncle, called Antonio—
I pray thee, mark me (that a brother should
Be so perfidious!)— he whom next thyself
Of all the world I loved and to him put
The manage of my state, as at that time
Through all the signories it was the first,
And Prospero the prime duke, being so reputed
In dignity, and for the liberal arts
Without a parallel. Those being all my study,
The government I cast upon my brother
And to my state grew stranger, being transported
And rapt in secret studies. Thy false uncle—
Dost thou attend me?

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text: Sir, most heedfully.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Being once perfected how to grant suits,
How to deny them, who t' advance and who
To trash for overtopping, new created
The creatures that were mine, I say —or changed 'em,
Or else new formed 'em—having both the key
Of officer and office, set all hearts i' th' state
To what tune pleased his ear, that now he was
The ivy which had hid my princely trunk,
And sucked my verdure out on ’t. Thou attend’st not.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text: O, good sir, I do.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
I pray thee, mark me.
I, thus neglecting worldly ends, all dedicated
To closeness and the bettering of my mind
With that which, but by being so retired,
O'erprized all popular rate, in my false brother
Awaked an evil nature. And my trust,
Like a good parent, did beget of him
A falsehood in its contrary as great
As my trust was, which had indeed no limit,
A confidence sans bound. He being thus lorded,
Not only with what my revenue yielded
But what my power might else exact, like one
Who having into truth, by telling of it,
Made such a sinner of his memory
To credit his own lie— he did believe
He was indeed the duke, out o' th' substitution
And executing th' outward face of royalty,
With all prerogative: Hence his ambition growing—
Dost thou hear?

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text: Your tale, sir, would cure deafness.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text: Your tale, sir, would cure deafness.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
To have no screen between this part he played
And him he played it for, he needs will be
Absolute Milan. Me, poor man, my library
Was dukedom large enough. Of temporal royalties
He thinks me now incapable, confederates—
So dry he was for sway—wi' th' King of Naples
To give him annual tribute, do him homage,
Subject his coronet to his crown and bend
The dukedom yet unbowed— alas, poor Milan!—
To most ignoble stooping.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text: Oh, the heavens!

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Mark his condition and the event. Then tell me
If this might be a brother.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Mark his condition and the event. Then tell me
If this might be a brother.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
I should sin
To think but nobly of my grandmother.
Good wombs have borne bad sons.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
I should sin
To think but nobly of my grandmother.
Good wombs have borne bad sons.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Now the condition.
The King of Naples, being an enemy
To me inveterate, hearkens my brother’s suit,
Which was that he, in lieu o' th' premises
Of homage and I know not how much tribute,
Should presently extirpate me and mine
Out of the dukedom, and confer fair Milan
With all the honors on my brother. Whereon,
A treacherous army levied, one midnight
Fated to th' purpose did Antonio open
The gates of Milan, and, i' th' dead of darkness,
The ministers for th' purpose hurried thence
Me and thy crying self.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
Alack, for pity!
I, not remembering how I cried out then,
Will cry it o'er again. It is a hint
That wrings mine eyes to ’t.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Hear a little further
And then I’ll bring thee to the present business
Which now ’s upon ’s, without the which this story
Were most impertinent.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
Wherefore did they not
That hour destroy us?

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Well demanded, wench.
My tale provokes that question. Dear, they durst not,
So dear the love my people bore me, nor set
A mark so bloody on the business, but
With colors fairer painted their foul ends.
In few, they hurried us aboard a bark,
Bore us some leagues to sea, where they prepared
A rotten carcass of a butt, not rigged,
Nor tackle, sail, nor mast. The very rats
Instinctively had quit it. There they hoist us
To cry to th' sea that roared to us, to sigh
To th' winds whose pity, sighing back again,
Did us but loving wrong.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
Alack, what trouble
Was I then to you!

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
Alack, what trouble
Was I then to you!

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Oh, a cherubim
Thou wast that did preserve me. Thou didst smile
Infusèd with a fortitude from heaven,
When I have decked the sea with drops full salt,
Under my burthen groaned; which raised in me
An undergoing stomach to bear up
Against what should ensue.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text: How came we ashore?

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
By providence divine.
Some food we had and some fresh water that
A noble Neapolitan, Gonzalo,
Out of his charity, who being then appointed
Master of this design, did give us, with
Rich garments, linens, stuffs, and necessaries,
Which since have steaded much. So, of his gentleness,
Knowing I loved my books, he furnished me
From mine own library with volumes that
I prize above my dukedom.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
Would I might
But ever see that man!

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Now I arise.
[stands and puts on his mantle]
Sit still, and hear the last of our sea-sorrow.
Here in this island we arrived, and here
Have I, thy schoolmaster, made thee more profit
Than other princesses can that have more time
For vainer hours and tutors not so careful.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
Heavens thank you for ’t! And now, I pray you, sir—
For still ’tis beating in my mind—your reason
For raising this sea storm?

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Know thus far forth:
By accident most strange, bountiful Fortune
(Now my dear lady) hath mine enemies
Brought to this shore. And by my prescience
I find my zenith doth depend upon
A most auspicious star, whose influence
If now I court not but omit, my fortunes
Will ever after droop. Here cease more questions.
Thou art inclined to sleep. 'Tis a good dullness,
And give it way. I know thou canst not choose.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Come
away, servant, come. I am ready now.
Approach, my Ariel, come.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Come
away, servant, come. I am ready now.
Approach, my Ariel, come.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Come
away, servant, come. I am ready now.
Approach, my Ariel, come.
ARIEL enters.

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text:
All hail, great master! Grave sir, hail! I come
To answer thy best pleasure, be ’t to fly,
To swim, to dive into the fire, to ride
On the curled clouds. To thy strong bidding, task
Ariel and all his quality.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Hast thou, spirit,
Performed to point the tempest that I bade thee?

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text:
To every article.
I boarded the king’s ship. Now on the beak,
Now in the waist, the deck, in every cabin,
I flamed amazement. Sometime I’d divide,
And burn in many places. On the topmast,
The yards, and bowsprit would I flame distinctly,
Then meet and join. Jove’s lightning, the precursors
O' th' dreadful thunderclaps, more momentary
And sight-outrunning were not. The fire and cracks
Of sulfurous roaring the most mighty Neptune
Seem to besiege and make his bold waves tremble,
Yea, his dread trident shake.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
My brave spirit!
Who was so firm, so constant, that this coil
Would not infect his reason?

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
My brave spirit!
Who was so firm, so constant, that this coil
Would not infect his reason?

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text:
Not a soul
But felt a fever of the mad and played
Some tricks of desperation. All but mariners
Plunged in the foaming brine and quit the vessel,
Then all afire with me. The king’s son, Ferdinand,
With hair up-staring—then, like reeds, not hair—
Was the first man that leaped, cried, “Hell is empty
And all the devils are here.”

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Why, that’s my spirit!
But was not this nigh shore?

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Why, that’s my spirit!
But was not this nigh shore?

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text: Close by, my master.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text: But are they, Ariel, safe?

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text:
Not a hair perished.
On their sustaining garments not a blemish,
But fresher than before. And, as thou badest me,
In troops I have dispersed them 'bout the isle.
The king’s son have I landed by himself,
Whom I left cooling of the air with sighs
In an odd angle of the isle, and sitting,
His arms in this sad knot.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Of the king’s ship,
The mariners, say how thou hast disposed,
And all the rest o' th' fleet.

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text:
Safely in harbor
Is the king’s ship. In the deep nook where once
Thou called’st me up at midnight to fetch dew
From the still-vexed Bermoothes, there she’s hid.
The mariners all under hatches stowed,
Who, with a charm joined to their suffered labor,
I have left asleep. And for the rest o' th' fleet,
Which I dispersed, they all have met again
And are upon the Mediterranean float,
Bound sadly home for Naples,
Supposing that they saw the king’s ship wracked
And his great person perish.

sentences.text:
Ariel, thy charge
Exactly is performed. But there’s more work.
What is the time o' th' day?

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO

Ariel, thy charge
Exactly is performed. But there’s more work.
What is the time o' th' day?

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text: Past the mid season.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
At least two glasses. The time ’twixt six and now
Must by us both be spent most preciously.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
At least two glasses. The time ’twixt six and now
Must by us both be spent most preciously.

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text:
Is there more toil? Since thou dost give me pains,
Let me remember thee what thou hast promised,
Which is not yet performed me.

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
How now? Moody?
What is ’t thou canst demand?

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
How now? Moody?
What is ’t thou canst demand?

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
How now? Moody?
What is ’t thou canst demand?

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text: My liberty.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text: Before the time be out? No more!

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text: Before the time be out? No more!

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text:
I prithee,
Remember I have done thee worthy service,
Told thee no lies, made thee no mistakings, served
Without or grudge or grumblings. Thou didst promise
To bate me a full year.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Dost thou forget
From what a torment I did free thee?

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text: No.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Thou dost, and think’st it much to tread the ooze
Of the salt deep,
To run upon the sharp wind of the north,
To do me business in the veins o' th' earth
When it is baked with frost.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO

Thou dost, and think’st it much to tread the ooze
Of the salt deep,
To run upon the sharp wind of the north,
To do me business in the veins o' th' earth
When it is baked with frost.

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text: I do not, sir.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Thou liest, malignant thing! Hast thou forgot
The foul witch Sycorax, who with age and envy
Was grown into a hoop? Hast thou forgot her?

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:

The foul witch Sycorax, who with age and envy
Was grown into a hoop? Hast thou forgot her?

Was grown into a hoop? Hast thou forgot her?

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text: No, sir.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text: Thou hast. Where was she born? Speak. Tell me.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text: Thou hast. Where was she born? Speak. Tell me.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text: Thou hast. Where was she born? Speak. Tell me.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text: Thou hast. Where was she born? Speak. Tell me.

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text: Sir, in Argier.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Oh, was she so? I must
Once in a month recount what thou hast been,
Which thou forget’st. This damned witch Sycorax,
For mischiefs manifold and sorceries terrible
To enter human hearing, from Argier,
Thou know’st, was banished. For one thing she did
They would not take her life. Is not this true?

Oh, was she so? I must

Which thou forget’st. This damned witch Sycorax,
For mischiefs manifold and sorceries terrible
To enter human hearing, from Argier,
Thou know’st, was banished. For one thing she did
They would not take her life. Is not this true?

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Oh, was she so? I must

Which thou forget’st. This damned witch Sycorax,

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text: Ay, sir.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
This blue-eyed hag was hither brought with child
And here was left by th' sailors. Thou, my slave,
As thou report’st thyself, wast then her servant.
And, for thou wast a spirit too delicate
To act her earthy and abhorred commands,
Refusing her grand hests, she did confine thee,
By help of her more potent ministers
And in her most unmitigable rage,
Into a cloven pine, within which rift
Imprisoned thou didst painfully remain
A dozen years; within which space she died
And left thee there, where thou didst vent thy groans
As fast as mill wheels strike. Then was this island—
Save for the son that she did litter here,
A freckled whelp hag-born—not honored with
A human shape.

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text: Yes, Caliban, her son.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Dull thing, I say so. He, that Caliban
Whom now I keep in service. Thou best know’st
What torment I did find thee in. Thy groans
Did make wolves howl and penetrate the breasts
Of ever angry bears. It was a torment
To lay upon the damned, which Sycorax
Could not again undo. It was mine art,
When I arrived and heard thee, that made gape
The pine and let thee out.

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text: I thank thee, master.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
If thou more murmur’st, I will rend an oak
And peg thee in his knotty entrails till
Thou hast howled away twelve winters.

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text:
Pardon, master.
I will be correspondent to command
And do my spiriting gently.

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text:
Pardon, master.
I will be correspondent to command
And do my spiriting gently.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Do so, and after two days
I will discharge thee.

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text:
That’s my noble master!
What shall I do? Say, what? What shall I do?

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Go make thyself like a nymph o' th' sea. Be subject
To no sight but thine and mine, invisible
To every eyeball else. Go take this shape
And hither come in ’t. Go hence with diligence.

[to MIRANDA]
Awake, dear heart, awake! Thou hast slept well.
Awake!

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
[waking] The strangeness of your story put
Heaviness in me.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Shake it off. Come on.
We’ll visit Caliban, my slave who never
Yields us kind answer.

'Tis a villain, sir,
I do not love to look on.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
But as ’tis,
We cannot miss him. He does make our fire,
Fetch in our wood, and serves in offices
That profit us. What, ho! Slave! Caliban!
Thou earth, thou! Speak.

sentences.speaker: CALIBAN
sentences.text: [within] There’s wood enough within.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO

Come forth, I say! There’s other business for thee.
Come, thou tortoise! When?

Come forth, I say! There’s other business for thee.
Come, thou tortoise! When?
ARIEL enters, in the form of a water nymph.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Fine apparition! My quaint Ariel,
Hark in thine ear. [whispers to ARIEL]

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Fine apparition! My quaint Ariel,
Hark in thine ear. [whispers to ARIEL]

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text: My lord it shall be done.
ARIEL exits.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
[to CALIBAN] Thou poisonous slave, got by the devil
himself
Upon thy wicked dam, come forth!
CALIBAN enters.

sentences.speaker: CALIBAN
sentences.text:
As wicked dew as e'er my mother brushed
With raven’s feather from unwholesome fen
Drop on you both! A southwest blow on ye
And blister you all o'er!

sentences.speaker: CALIBAN
sentences.text:
As wicked dew as e'er my mother brushed
With raven’s feather from unwholesome fen
Drop on you both! A southwest blow on ye
And blister you all o'er!

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
For this, be sure, tonight thou shalt have cramps,
Side-stitches that shall pen thy breath up. Urchins
Shall, forth at vast of night that they may work,
All exercise on thee. Thou shalt be pinched
As thick as honeycomb, each pinch more stinging
Than bees that made 'em.

sentences.speaker: CALIBAN
sentences.text:
I must eat my dinner.
This island’s mine, by Sycorax my mother,
Which thou takest from me. When thou camest first,
Thou strok’st me and made much of me, wouldst give me
Water with berries in ’t, and teach me how
To name the bigger light, and how the less,
That burn by day and night. And then I loved thee
And showed thee all the qualities o' th' isle,
The fresh springs, brine pits, barren place and
fertile.
Cursed be I that did so! All the charms
Of Sycorax, toads, beetles, bats, light on you!
For I am all the subjects that you have,
Which first was mine own king. And here you sty me
In this hard rock, whiles you do keep from me
The rest o' th' island.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Thou most lying slave,
Whom stripes may move, not kindness! I have used thee,
Filth as thou art, with human care, and lodged thee
In mine own cell till thou didst seek to violate
The honor of my child.

sentences.speaker: CALIBAN
sentences.text:
Oh ho, oh ho! Would ’t had been done!
Thou didst prevent me. I had peopled else
This isle with Calibans.

sentences.speaker: CALIBAN
sentences.text:

sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
Abhorrèd slave,
Which any print of goodness wilt not take,
Being capable of all ill! I pitied thee,
Took pains to make thee speak, taught thee each hour
One thing or other. When thou didst not, savage,
Know thine own meaning, but wouldst gabble like
A thing most brutish, I endowed thy purposes
With words that made them known. But thy vile race,
Though thou didst learn, had that in ’t which good
natures
Could not abide to be with. Therefore wast thou
Deservedly confined into this rock,
Who hadst deserved more than a prison.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
Abhorrèd slave,
Which any print of goodness wilt not take,
Being capable of all ill! I pitied thee,
Took pains to make thee speak, taught thee each hour
One thing or other. When thou didst not, savage,
Know thine own meaning, but wouldst gabble like
A thing most brutish, I endowed thy purposes
With words that made them known. But thy vile race,
Though thou didst learn, had that in ’t which good
natures
Could not abide to be with. Therefore wast thou
Deservedly confined into this rock,
Who hadst deserved more than a prison.

sentences.speaker: CALIBAN
sentences.text:
You taught me language, and my profit on ’t
Is I know how to curse. The red plague rid you
For learning me your language!

sentences.speaker: CALIBAN
sentences.text:
You taught me language, and my profit on ’t
Is I know how to curse. The red plague rid you
For learning me your language!

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Hag-seed, hence!
Fetch us in fuel. And be quick, thou 'rt best,
To answer other business. Shrug’st thou, malice?
If thou neglect’st or dost unwillingly
What I command, I’ll rack thee with old cramps,
Fill all thy bones with aches, make thee roar
That beasts shall tremble at thy din.

It would control my dam’s god, Setebos,
And make a vassal of him.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text: So, slave, hence!
CALIBAN exits.
FERDINAND enters with ARIEL, who is invisible and is playing music and singing.

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text:
[sings]
Come unto these yellow sands,
And then take hands.
Curtsied when you have, and kissed
The wild waves whist.
Foot it featly here and there,
And, sweet sprites, bear
The burden. Hark, hark!

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text:
Hark, hark! I hear
The strain of strutting chanticleer
Cry “Cock-a-diddle-dow.”

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: FERDINAND
sentences.text:
Where should this music be? I' th' air or th' earth?
It sounds no more, and sure, it waits upon
Some god o' th' island. Sitting on a bank,
Weeping again the king my father’s wrack,
This music crept by me upon the waters,
Allaying both their fury and my passion
With its sweet air. Thence I have followed it,
Or it hath drawn me rather. But ’tis gone.
No, it begins again.

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text:
[sings]
Full fathom five thy father lies.
Of his bones are coral made.
Those are pearls that were his eyes.
Nothing of him that doth fade,
But doth suffer a sea-change
Into something rich and strange.
Sea nymphs hourly ring his knell.

sentences.speaker: FERDINAND
sentences.text:
The ditty does remember my drowned father.
This is no mortal business, nor no sound
That the earth owes. I hear it now above me.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
[to MIRANDA] The fringèd curtains of thine eye advance
And say what thou seest yond.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
What is ’t? A spirit?
Lord, how it looks about! Believe me, sir,
It carries a brave form. But ’tis a spirit.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
No, wench! It eats and sleeps and hath such senses
As we have, such. This gallant which thou seest
Was in the wrack. And, but he’s something stained
With grief that’s beauty’s canker, thou mightst call
him
A goodly person. He hath lost his fellows
And strays about to find 'em.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
I might call him
A thing divine, for nothing natural
I ever saw so noble.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
[aside] It goes on, I see,
As my soul prompts it. Spirit, fine spirit! I’ll free
thee
Within two days for this.

sentences.speaker: FERDINAND
sentences.text:
[seeing MIRANDA] Most sure, the goddess
On whom these airs attend! Vouchsafe my prayer
May know if you remain upon this island,
And that you will some good instruction give
How I may bear me here. My prime request,
Which I do last pronounce, is, O you wonder!
If you be maid or no.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA

No wonder, sir,
But certainly a maid.

sentences.speaker: FERDINAND
sentences.text:
My language! Heavens,
I am the best of them that speak this speech,
Were I but where ’tis spoken.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
How? The best?
What wert thou if the King of Naples heard thee?

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
How? The best?
What wert thou if the King of Naples heard thee?

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
How? The best?
What wert thou if the King of Naples heard thee?

sentences.speaker: FERDINAND
sentences.text:
A single thing, as I am now, that wonders
To hear thee speak of Naples. He does hear me,
And that he does I weep. Myself am Naples,
Who with mine eyes, never since at ebb, beheld
The king my father wracked.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text: Alack, for mercy!

sentences.speaker: FERDINAND
sentences.text:
Yes, faith, and all his lords, the Duke of Milan
And his brave son being twain.

sentences.speaker: FERDINAND
sentences.text:
Yes, faith, and all his lords, the Duke of Milan
And his brave son being twain.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
[aside] The Duke of Milan
And his more braver daughter could control thee
If now ’twere fit to do ’t! At the first sight
They have changed eyes. Delicate Ariel,
I’ll set thee free for this.
[to FERDINAND]
A word, good sir.
I fear you have done yourself some wrong. A word.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
[ aside] Why speaks my father so ungently? This
Is the third man that e'er I saw, the first
That e'er I sighed for. Pity move my father
To be inclined my way!

sentences.speaker: FERDINAND
sentences.text:
[to MIRANDA]
Oh, if a virgin,
And your affection not gone forth, I’ll make you
The queen of Naples.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Soft, sir! One word more.
[ aside]
They are both in either’s powers, but this swift
business
I must uneasy make lest too light winning
Make the prize light.
[To FERDINAND]
One word more. I charge thee
That thou attend me. Thou dost here usurp
The name thou owest not, and hast put thyself
Upon this island as a spy to win it
From me, the lord on ’t.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
There’s nothing ill can dwell in such a temple.
If the ill spirit have so fair a house,
Good things will strive to dwell with ’t.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
[to FERDINAND] Follow me.
[to MIRANDA] Speak not you for him. He’s a traitor.
[to FERDINAND] Come,
I’ll manacle thy neck and feet together.
Seawater shalt thou drink. Thy food shall be
The fresh-brook muscles, withered roots, and husks
Wherein the acorn cradled. Follow.

Seawater shalt thou drink. Thy food shall be
The fresh-brook muscles, withered roots, and husks
Wherein the acorn cradled. Follow.

No.
I will resist such entertainment till
Mine enemy has more power.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
O dear father,
Make not too rash a trial of him, for
He’s gentle and not fearful.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
What, I say?
My foot my tutor?— Put thy sword up, traitor,
Who makest a show but darest not strike, thy conscience
Is so possessed with guilt. Come from thy ward,
For I can here disarm thee with this stick
And make thy weapon drop.

sentences.text: Beseech you, father.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text: Hence! Hang not on my garments.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text: Hence! Hang not on my garments.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
Sir, have pity,
I’ll be his surety.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
Sir, have pity,
I’ll be his surety.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
Silence! One word more
Shall make me chide thee, if not hate thee. What,
An advocate for an imposter? Hush,
Thou think’st there is no more such shapes as he,
Having seen but him and Caliban. Foolish wench,
To th' most of men this is a Caliban
And they to him are angels.

sentences.text:
My affections
Are then most humble. I have no ambition
To see a goodlier man.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
[to FERDINAND] Come on. Obey.
Thy nerves are in their infancy again
And have no vigor in them.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
[to FERDINAND] Come on. Obey.

And have no vigor in them.

sentences.speaker: FERDINAND
sentences.text:
So they are.
My spirits, as in a dream, are all bound up.
My father’s loss, the weakness which I feel,
The wrack of all my friends, nor this man’s threats,
To whom I am subdued, are but light to me,
Might I but through my prison once a day
Behold this maid. All corners else o' th' earth
Let liberty make use of. Space enough
Have I in such a prison.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
[aside] It works!
[to FERDINAND] Come on.
[aside] Thou hast done well, fine Ariel!
[to FERDINAND] Follow me.
[to ARIEL] Hark what thou else shalt do me.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
[to FERDINAND] Be of comfort.
My father’s of a better nature, sir,
Than he appears by speech. This is unwonted
Which now came from him.

sentences.speaker: MIRANDA
sentences.text:
[to FERDINAND] Be of comfort.

Than he appears by speech. This is unwonted
Which now came from him.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
[to ARIEL] Thou shalt be free
As mountain winds. But then exactly do
All points of my command.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
[to ARIEL] Thou shalt be free
As mountain winds. But then exactly do
All points of my command.

sentences.speaker: ARIEL
sentences.text: To th' syllable.

sentences.speaker: PROSPERO
sentences.text:
[to FERDINAND] Come, follow.
[to MIRANDA] Speak not for him.
They exit.
ALONSO, SEBASTIAN, ANTONIO, GONZALO, ADRIAN, FRANCISCO, and others enter.

sentences.speaker: GONZALO
sentences.text:
[to ALONSO]
Beseech you, sir, be merry. You have cause,
So have we all, of joy, for our escape
Is much beyond our loss. Our hint of woe
Is common. Every day some sailor’s wife,
The masters of some merchant, and the merchant
Have just our theme of woe. But for the miracle—
I mean our preservation—few in millions
Can speak like us. Then wisely, good sir, weigh
Our sorrow with our comfort.
